<!-- ★ Inline branding injector — runs BEFORE all other JS (~20K lines).
     Reads cached branding from localStorage and patches the loader DOM instantly
     so the user never sees the default "A" / "Atlas Bank" flash. -->
<script>
(function(){
  try {
    var c = localStorage.getItem('atlas_branding');
    if (!c) return;
    var b = JSON.parse(c);
    var m = document.querySelector('.loader-brand-mark');
    var n = document.querySelector('.loader-text h1');
    if (m) {
      if (b.logo) m.innerHTML = '<img src="'+b.logo+'" style="width:100%;height:100%;object-fit:cover;border-radius:16px" alt="Logo">';
      else m.textContent = (b.bankName||'A').charAt(0);
      if (b.primaryColor && !b.logo) m.style.background = 'linear-gradient(135deg,'+b.primaryColor+','+(b.accentColor||'#67e8b5')+')';
    }
    if (n) n.textContent = (b.bankNameShort||b.bankName||'Atlas Bank') + ' Enterprise';
    if (b.primaryColor) {
      document.querySelectorAll('.loader-ring').forEach(function(r,i){
        r.style.borderTopColor = i===0 ? b.primaryColor : (i===1 ? (b.accentColor||'#67e8b5') : '#ffbe69');
      });
    }
  } catch(e){}
})();
</script>

          <strong>Included:</strong> Fixed amount covers both principal + interest (e.g., pay 1,000,000 total).

        </div>
      </div>

<script src="https://cdn.sheetjs.com/xlsx-0.20.3/package/dist/xlsx.full.min.js" crossorigin="anonymous"></script>
<script src="https://unpkg.com/lucide@0.468.0/dist/umd/lucide.js" crossorigin="anonymous"></script>
<script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/dompurify/3.1.6/purify.min.js" crossorigin="anonymous"></script>
<script>
/* ================================================================
   API LAYER — Connects frontend to PHP/MySQL backend
   ================================================================ */
const API_BASE = '/atlas-bank-backend/api/';  // Must include the backend subdirectory — .htaccess routing lives there
let authToken = localStorage.getItem('atlas_token') || '';
let csrfToken = '';  // CSRF token from backend — refreshed on each mutating request
let API_CONNECTED = true;   // Set to false to use offline/demo mode

/* ================================================================
   ICON HELPER — Enterprise-grade Lucide icon rendering
   Returns inline SVG string for use in dynamic HTML generation.
   Usage: IC('search', 18) or IC('trending-up')
   Note: lucide.icons uses PascalCase keys; this helper converts
   kebab-case input (e.g. 'alert-triangle') to PascalCase for lookup.
   ================================================================ */
function IC(name, size = 18, extraClass = '') {
  try {
    if (typeof lucide !== 'undefined' && lucide.icons) {
      // Convert kebab-case to PascalCase: 'alert-triangle' -> 'AlertTriangle'
      const pascal = name.replace(/(^|[\s-_]+)(\w)/g, function(m, sep, c) { return c.toUpperCase(); });
      const iconNode = lucide.icons[pascal];
      if (iconNode) {
        return '<span class="lucide-icon-wrap' + (extraClass ? ' ' + extraClass : '') + '" style="display:inline-flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;width:' + size + 'px;height:' + size + 'px;flex-shrink:0">' +
          iconNode.toSvg({ width: size, height: size, strokeWidth: 1.8 }) + '</span>';
      }
    }
  } catch(e) {}
  return '<span style="display:inline-flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;width:' + size + 'px;height:' + size + 'px;flex-shrink:0;font-size:' + (size * 0.55) + 'rem;color:var(--muted)">·</span>';
}
/** Re-initialize Lucide icons after dynamic DOM changes */
function refreshIcons(root) {
  try { lucide.createIcons(root || undefined); } catch(e) {}
}


// Fetch a fresh CSRF token from the backend (GET /api/auth returns a new token)
// Uses the API helper to ensure cookies are sent and rotated tokens are captured.
// Falls back to raw fetch with retry if the API helper is not yet ready.
// ★ Deduplication lock: prevents concurrent refreshes from causing race conditions ★
// ★ force=true: tells auth.php to generate a NEW token (used after 403 expired errors)
let _csrfRefreshPromise = null;
async function refreshCsrfToken(force = false) {
  // If a refresh is already in-flight, return the same promise (deduplication)
  if (_csrfRefreshPromise) return _csrfRefreshPromise;
  _csrfRefreshPromise = (async () => {
    try {
      // Use the API.get helper which properly sends credentials and reads rotated tokens
      const data = await API.get('auth' + (force ? '?force_csrf=1' : ''));
      if (data?.data?.csrf_token) {
        csrfToken = data.data.csrf_token;
        return true;
      }
    } catch(e) {
      console.warn('[CSRF] API.get refresh failed, trying raw fetch:', e.message);
    }
    // Fallback: raw fetch (shouldn't normally be needed, but covers edge cases)
    try {
      const resp = await fetch(API_BASE + 'auth' + (force ? '?force_csrf=1' : ''), {
        headers: { 'Authorization': 'Bearer ' + authToken, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        credentials: 'include'
      });
      if (!resp.ok) {
        console.warn('[CSRF] Session endpoint returned ' + resp.status + ', token may be stale.');
        return false;
      }
      const data = await resp.json();
      if (data?.data?.csrf_token) {
        csrfToken = data.data.csrf_token;
        return true;
      }
    } catch(e) {
      console.error('[CSRF] Failed to refresh CSRF token:', e.message);
    }
    return false;
  })().finally(() => { _csrfRefreshPromise = null; });
  return _csrfRefreshPromise;
}

// ★ CSRF-Safe Mutation Queue ★
// Prevents race conditions between concurrent POST/PUT/DELETE requests.
// Without this, the auto-deduction engine's syncTransactionToBackend() can rotate
// the CSRF token between a user action's refreshCsrfToken() and its POST,
// causing a 403 Forbidden on the user's request.
// The queue serializes all mutations: refresh → send → capture rotated token → next mutation.
// ★ FIX: Each mutation now refreshes CSRF before sending, guaranteeing the token
// is always the latest from the session. External refreshCsrfToken() calls have
// been removed from syncTransactionToBackend and autoDeductTick to prevent interference.
let _mutationQueue = Promise.resolve();
function enqueueMutation(requestFn) {
  _mutationQueue = _mutationQueue.then(async () => {
    // ★ Only refresh CSRF when authenticated — no session exists before login,
    // so refreshing would wastefully call GET /api/auth and get { authenticated: false }.
    // After login, authToken is set and the queue refreshes CSRF for each mutation.
    if (authToken) { try { await refreshCsrfToken(); } catch(e) {} }
    return requestFn();
  }).catch(err => {
    console.warn('[API] Mutation error (queue continues):', err);
    return null;
  });
  return _mutationQueue;
}

// API Helper - makes fetch calls to the PHP backend
const API = {
  async request(endpoint, options = {}) {
    const isMutation = options.method && options.method !== 'GET' && options.method !== 'HEAD';
    if (isMutation) {
      // Serialize all mutating requests through the mutation queue.
      // Each mutation: refresh CSRF → send request → capture rotated token.
      // This guarantees no concurrent mutations can cause token race conditions.
      return enqueueMutation(() => this._doRequest(endpoint, options));
    }
    // GET/HEAD requests go through directly (no CSRF needed, no serialization)
    return this._doRequest(endpoint, options);
  },

  async _doRequest(endpoint, options = {}) {
    const url = API_BASE + endpoint;
    const isMutation = options.method && options.method !== 'GET' && options.method !== 'HEAD';
    let headers = { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...(options.headers || {}) };
    if (authToken) headers['Authorization'] = 'Bearer ' + authToken;
    // Send current CSRF token for mutations — DO NOT refresh here.
    // The mutation queue ensures serialization: each mutation uses the rotated
    // token from the previous mutation's response header. Refreshing here would
    // cause an extra GET that can return a stale token and waste a round-trip.
    if (isMutation && csrfToken) headers['X-CSRF-Token'] = csrfToken;
    try {
      const _controller = new AbortController();
      const _timeout = setTimeout(() => _controller.abort(), 30000); // 30s timeout
      let resp = await fetch(url, { ...options, headers, credentials: 'include', signal: _controller.signal });
      clearTimeout(_timeout);
      // Read the rotated CSRF token from the response header (sent after successful validation)
      const newCsrf = resp.headers.get('X-CSRF-Token');
      if (newCsrf) csrfToken = newCsrf;
      let data = await resp.json();
      // ★ Auto-retry on CSRF 403: force-generate a fresh token and retry once ★
      // ★ FIX: Bypass refreshCsrfToken() deduplication lock — use direct fetch to ensure
      // we always get a FRESH token. Previously, if a non-force refresh was in-flight,
      // the force parameter was ignored, causing the retry to reuse a stale token → 403 again.
      if (resp.status === 403 && (data.error || '').toLowerCase().includes('csrf')) {
        console.warn('[API] CSRF validation failed, force-refreshing token via direct fetch and retrying...');
        try {
          const authResp = await fetch(API_BASE + 'auth?force_csrf=1', {
            headers: authToken ? { 'Authorization': 'Bearer ' + authToken, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } : { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
            credentials: 'include'
          });
          if (authResp.ok) {
            const authData = await authResp.json();
            if (authData?.data?.csrf_token) {
              csrfToken = authData.data.csrf_token;
              headers['X-CSRF-Token'] = csrfToken;
              console.warn('[API] Got fresh CSRF token, retrying mutation...');
              resp = await fetch(url, { ...options, headers, credentials: 'include' });
              const retryCsrf = resp.headers.get('X-CSRF-Token');
              if (retryCsrf) csrfToken = retryCsrf;
              data = await resp.json();
              if (!resp.ok) {
                const msg = data.error || data.message || `HTTP ${resp.status}`;
                console.error(`[API] RETRY ${options.method || 'GET'} ${url} → ${resp.status}: ${msg}`);
                showToast('error', `API ${resp.status}`, msg);
                return null;
              }
              return data;
            }
          }
        } catch(retryErr) {
          console.error('[API] CSRF retry fetch failed:', retryErr);
        }
      }
      if (!resp.ok) {
        const msg = data.error || data.message || `HTTP ${resp.status}`;
        // 403 "Access denied" from requireModule() is expected for restricted users — stay silent
        const isModuleDenied = resp.status === 403 && (msg.toLowerCase().includes('access denied') || msg.toLowerCase().includes('do not have permission'));
        if (isModuleDenied) {
          return null;
        }
        // 403 Password change required — redirect to profile
        // ★ FIX: Only show the modal/toast ONCE. Use _passwordChangeModalShown guard
        // to prevent the infinite loop where every blocked mutation (audit, auto-deduct,
        // notification poll) re-triggers openProfileModal('security') and showToast,
        // which disrupts user input in the password fields.
        if (resp.status === 403 && (data.requires_password_change || resp.headers.get('X-Password-Change-Required'))) {
          if (!STATE._passwordChangeModalShown) {
            STATE._passwordChangeModalShown = true;
            STATE._forcePasswordChange = true;
            showToast('warning', 'Password Change Required', 'Please update your password before continuing.');
            try { openProfileModal('security'); } catch(e) {}
          }
          return null;
        }
        // 401 = unauthenticated — but distinguish between login failure and session expiry.
        // POST /api/auth returning 401 = wrong credentials (login attempt), NOT session expiry.
        // Only treat as session expiry if there IS an active token that became invalid.
        if (resp.status === 401) {
          const isLoginAttempt = url.endsWith('/auth');
          if (!isLoginAttempt && authToken) {
            // Genuine session expiry — token was valid but is now rejected
            authToken = '';
            csrfToken = '';
            localStorage.removeItem('atlas_token');
            STATE.currentUser = null;
            clearInterval(STATE.sessionTimerInterval);
            clearInterval(STATE.autoDeductInterval);
            stopNotificationPolling();
            stopAuditAutoRefresh();
            stopBranchAutoRefresh();
            $('appShell').style.display = 'none';
            $('loginScreen').style.display = 'flex';
            showToast('warning','Session Expired','Your session has expired. Please sign in again.');
          }
          // For login attempts, fall through to the general error handler below
          // which shows the actual error message (e.g. "Invalid username or password")
          if (isLoginAttempt) {
            const msg = data.error || data.message || 'Invalid username or password.';
            showToast('error', 'Login Failed', msg);
          }
          return null;
        }
        console.error(`[API] ${options.method || 'GET'} ${url} → ${resp.status}: ${msg}`);
        showToast('error', `API ${resp.status}`, msg);
        return null;
      }
      return data;
    } catch (err) {
      if (API_CONNECTED) { console.warn('API unavailable, using local cache:', err); API_CONNECTED = false; }
      return null;
    }
  },

  get(endpoint, params = {}) {
    const qs = new URLSearchParams(params).toString();
    return this.request(endpoint + (qs ? '?' + qs : ''));
  },
  post(endpoint, body) { return this.request(endpoint, { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify(body) }); },
  // Use POST + _method override for PUT/DELETE — Apache/mod_php doesn't always handle real PUT/DELETE
  put(endpoint, body) {
    const sep = endpoint.includes('?') ? '&' : '?';
    return this.request(endpoint + sep + '_method=PUT', { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify(body) });
  },
  del(endpoint) {
    const sep = endpoint.includes('?') ? '&' : '?';
    return this.request(endpoint + sep + '_method=DELETE', { method: 'POST' });
  }
};

// Helper: persist a local transaction to the backend.
// Returns a Promise so callers can await it for serialized DB operations.
// The backend transactions.php POST handler automatically updates account balances for POSTED transactions.
function syncTransactionToBackend(txn) {
  if (!API_CONNECTED) return Promise.resolve();
  // FIXED: Validate amount is a finite number — prevents NaN/Infinity from corrupting the DB
  const safeAmount = typeof txn.amount === 'number' && isFinite(txn.amount) ? txn.amount : 0;
  if (safeAmount <= 0 && txn.status === 'POSTED') {
    console.warn('[Sync] Skipping zero/negative amount transaction:', txn.ref, 'amount:', txn.amount);
    return Promise.resolve();
  }
  // ★ FIX: CSRF refresh is now handled by the mutation queue (enqueueMutation).
  // Removed refreshCsrfToken() call here — calling it outside the queue created
  // race conditions where this GET could return a token that gets stale before
  // the mutation executes. The queue guarantees refresh → send sequentially.
  return API.post('transactions', {
    type: txn.type,
    direction: txn.direction,
    amount: safeAmount,
    account: txn.account,
    account_type: txn.accountType || '',
    customer_name: txn.customerName || '',
    description: txn.description || '',
    category: txn.category || '',
    branch: txn.branch || '',
    status: txn.status || 'POSTED',
    memo: txn.memo || '',
    module: txn.module || 'Loans'
  }).then(result => {
    if (result) console.log('[Sync] Transaction', txn.ref, 'persisted to backend');
  });
}

/* ================================================================
   DATA LAYER — Local cache populated from API / fallback demo data
   ================================================================ */
let DB = {
  bankBranding: {

// ================================================================
// All transaction and account data is 100% database driven.
// Deposits, withdrawals, fees, and taxes are persisted via API.post()
// to the PHP/MySQL backend. On page refresh, loadAllData() fetches
// everything from the database. No localStorage fallback needed.
// ================================================================

// ================================================================
// DATA LOADER — Fetches all data from PHP API backend
// ================================================================
async function loadAllData() {
  if (!API_CONNECTED) { showToast('warning','Offline Mode','Running with demo data. Connect to the PHP backend for live data.'); return; }
  // Clear statement transaction cache on data refresh to prevent stale data
  STATE.statementTxnCache = {};
  STATE.plServerData = null;  // Clear P&L server cache to force fresh fetch
  
  // ★ RBAC-aware data loading: only call endpoints the user has module access to.
  // Endpoints with no module gate (branding, notifications, policies, operating-account,
  // loan-fund-accounts) are always called. Module-gated endpoints are conditionally
  // called based on the user's granted modules — this prevents 403 errors from
  // appearing in the console when a user is restricted from certain sections.
  // ★ FIX (DASH-012): Backend returns modules as [{name:'CUSTOMERS', access:'FULL'}, ...]
  // not plain strings. Normalize each module to a string before calling .toUpperCase().
  // ★ FIX (CUST-015): Wrap in String() to handle edge cases where m.name is a number
  // or other non-string type — prevents "toUpperCase is not a function" TypeError.
  const _modName = (m) => typeof m === 'string' ? m : String(m?.name || m?.module_name || '');
  const _has = (rbacKey) => {
    const mods = STATE.currentUser?.modules || [];
    if (mods.some(m => _modName(m) === 'ALL')) return true;
    const target = (rbacKey||'').toUpperCase();
    // ★ FIX: Use getModuleRbacKeys() which has the COMPLETE display-name → RBAC-key mapping.
    // Previously, this used a hardcoded `expansions` object that was MISSING entries like
    // 'Branch Management' → ['BRANCHES'], 'Dashboard' → ['DASHBOARD'], etc.
    // When admin stored modules as display names (e.g. 'Branch Management'), _has('BRANCHES')
    // returned false, so the branches API was NEVER called, and DB.branches stayed empty.
    // The admin branch dropdown only showed "All Branches" with no individual branches.
    return mods.some(m => {
      const mName = _modName(m);
      if (mName.toUpperCase() === target) return true;
      const keys = getModuleRbacKeys(mName);
      return keys.some(k => (k||'').toUpperCase() === target);
    });
  };
  const _skip = () => Promise.resolve(null);

  showToast('info','Syncing','Loading data from server...');
  const results = await Promise.allSettled([
    API.get('branding'),                                                          // [0] always (no module gate)
    _has('BRANCHES')          ? API.get('branches')            : _skip(),         // [1]
    _has('STAFF')             ? API.get('staff')               : _skip(),         // [2]
    _has('CUSTOMERS')         ? API.get('customers', {pageSize:500}) : _skip(),   // [3]
    _has('ACCOUNTS')          ? API.get('accounts', {pageSize:2000}) : _skip(),    // [4] ★ FIX (RA-ACC-001): Backend cap raised from MAX_PAGE_SIZE(100) to 2000
    _has('TRANSACTIONS')      ? API.get('transactions', {pageSize:2000}) : _skip(), // [5] ★ FIX (RA-CF-002/RA-TXN-002): Backend cap raised from 500 to 2000
    _has('LOANS')             ? API.get('loans', {pageSize:2000}) : _skip(),      // [6] ★ FIX (RA-GLA-002): Added pageSize — was loading only 20 (backend default), causing massive understatement of loan portfolio
    API.get('settings'),                                                           // [7] ALWAYS loaded — withdrawal fees, limits, session timeout etc. needed by all users
    _has('CHART_OF_ACCOUNTS') ? API.get('chart-of-accounts')   : _skip(),         // [8]
    _has('APPROVALS')         ? API.get('approvals')           : _skip(),         // [9]
    _has('AUDIT')             ? API.get('audit', {pageSize:500}) : _skip(),       // [10]
    API.get('notifications'),                                                   // [11] always (no module gate)
    API.get('policies'),                                                            // [12] always (no module gate)
    _has('EXPENSES')          ? API.get('expenses', {pageSize:2000}) : _skip(),   // [13] ★ FIX (RA-CF-001): Backend cap raised from MAX_PAGE_SIZE(100) to 2000
    _has('DOCUMENTS')         ? API.get('documents', {pageSize:500}) : _skip(),  // [14]
    _has('REPORTS')           ? API.get('reports', {type:'dashboard'}) : _skip(), // [15]
    _has('REPORTS')           ? API.get('reports', {type:'balance_trends'}) : _skip(), // [16]
    _has('PROFITLOSS')        ? API.get('reports', {type:'profit_loss'}) : _skip(), // [17]
    API.get('operating-account'),                                               // [18] always (no module gate)
    API.get('loan-fund-accounts'),                                              // [19] always (no module gate)
    _has('ACCOUNTS')          ? API.get('general-ledger')     : _skip(),         // [20] accounts + trial balance
    _has('ACCOUNTS')          ? API.get('general-ledger/entries', {pageSize:1000}) : _skip(), // [21] GL journal entries
    _has('LOANS')             ? API.get('loan-applications', {pageSize:500}) : _skip(), // [22] real loan applications + checks from DB
  ]);
  
  // Map results to DB cache
  if (results[0].status === 'fulfilled' && results[0].value?.data) {
    const b = results[0].value.data;
    DB.bankBranding = {
      bankName: b.bank_name||'ATLAS BANK', bankNameShort: b.bank_name_short||'Atlas Bank',
      tagline: b.tagline||'', logo: b.logo||'', primaryColor: b.primary_color||'#58b7ff',
      accentColor: b.accent_color||'#67e8b5', headOfficeAddress: b.head_office_address||'',
      phone: b.phone||'', phoneAlt: b.phone_alt||'', email: b.email||'',
      website: b.website||'', swiftCode: b.swift_code||'', cbnLicenseNumber: b.cbn_license_number||'',
      registrationNumber: b.registration_number||'', taxIdentificationNumber: b.tax_identification_number||'',
      slogan: b.slogan||''
    };
    // ★ Apply branding to loader immediately (before appShell renders)
    Loader.applyBranding(DB.bankBranding);
    // Cache branding for instant display on next page refresh
    Loader.cacheBranding(DB.bankBranding);
  }
  if (results[1].status === 'fulfilled' && results[1].value?.data) DB.branches = (results[1].value.data||[]).map(r => ({
    id:r.id, code:r.code, name:r.name, region:r.region||'', country:r.country||'CM', status:r.status,
    address:r.address||'', phone:r.phone||'', manager:r.manager||'', openedDate:r.opened_date||'',
    createdAt:r.created_at, updatedAt:r.updated_at
  }));
  if (results[2].status === 'fulfilled' && results[2].value?.data) DB.staff = results[2].value.data.map(s => ({
    id:s.id, username:s.username, fullName:s.full_name, initials:s.initials||'',
    role:s.role, department:s.department||'', email:s.email||'', phone:s.phone||'',
    position:s.position||'', branches:s.branches||[], modules:s.modules||[],
    approvalLimit:parseFloat(s.approval_limit||0), ipRestrictions:s.ip_restrictions||'',
    mfaRequired:!!s.mfa_required, employmentStatus:s.employment_status||'ACTIVE',
    lastLogin:s.last_login, profilePicture:s.profile_picture||'',
    deactivationReason:s.deactivation_reason||'', suspensionReason:s.suspension_reason||'',
    password:''
  }));
  if (results[3].status === 'fulfilled' && results[3].value?.data) DB.customers = results[3].value.data.map(c => ({
    id:c.id, customerNumber:c.customer_number, customerType:c.customer_type,
    fullName:c.full_name, status:c.status, riskRating:c.risk_rating,
    branch:c.branch||'', products:c.products||c.customer_products||[], relationshipStarted:c.relationship_started,
    nextAction:c.next_action||'', phone:c.phone||'', email:c.email||'',
    notes:c.notes||''
  }));
  if (results[4].status === 'fulfilled' && results[4].value?.data) DB.accounts = results[4].value.data.map(a => ({
    id:a.id, accountNumber:a.account_number, customerId:a.customer_id,
    customerName:a.customer_name||'', productType:a.product_type, branch:a.branch||'',
    status:a.status, currency:a.currency||'XAF', ledgerBalance:parseFloat(a.ledger_balance||0),
    availableBalance:parseFloat(a.available_balance||0), holdBalance:parseFloat(a.hold_balance||0),
    openedAt:a.opened_at, taxExemptions:a.tax_exemptions||[]
  }));
  // Account balances are updated atomically in the backend POST handler
  // (see transactions.php line 307-335) — no client-side override needed.
  if (results[5].status === 'fulfilled' && results[5].value?.data) DB.transactions = results[5].value.data.map(t => {
    const _deductions = t.deductions || [];
    // Reconstruct taxBreakdown from API deductions, falling back to memo-encoded data
    let _taxBreakdown = _deductions.map(d => ({
      label: d.deduction_name || d.label || '', rate: d.rate ? parseFloat(d.rate).toFixed(2) + '%' : '0.00%',
      amount: parseFloat(d.amount || 0), key: d.deduction_key || '', exempt: parseFloat(d.amount || 0) === 0
    }));
    // If no deductions from API but memo has encoded taxBreakdown, parse it
    if (_taxBreakdown.length === 0 && t.memo) {
      const { taxBreakdown: memoBreakdown, memo: cleanMemo } = parseMemoTaxBreakdown(t.memo);
      if (memoBreakdown.length > 0) _taxBreakdown = memoBreakdown;
    }
    return {
      id:t.id, ref:t.ref, type:t.type, status:t.status, branch:t.branch||'',
      account:t.account||'', accountType:t.account_type||'', customerName:t.customer_name||'',
      description:t.description||'', category:t.category||'', direction:t.direction,
      amount:parseFloat(t.amount||0), memo:t.memo||'', module:t.module||'',
      timestamp:t.created_at, fee:t.fee?parseFloat(t.fee):0, feePct:t.fee_pct?parseFloat(t.fee_pct):0,
      netAmount:t.net_amount!=null?parseFloat(t.net_amount):null,
      feeMode:t.fee_mode||'',
      totalTax:t.total_tax?parseFloat(t.total_tax):0,
      taxesEnabled:parseFloat(t.total_tax||0)>0,
      taxBreakdown:_taxBreakdown,
      deductions:_deductions,
      _parentId:t.parent_id||null  // ★ FIX (TXN-002): Map parent_id from backend for reliable child linking
    };
  });
  // Transactions are fetched directly from the database — 100% database driven.
  if (results[6].status === 'fulfilled' && results[6].value?.data) DB.loans = results[6].value.data.map(l => ({
    id:l.id,
    loanNumber: sanitizeLoanNumber(l.loan_number),
    customerId:l.customer_id, customerName:l.customer_name||'',
    branch:l.branch||'', status:l.status, principal:parseFloat(l.principal||0),
    outstanding:parseFloat(l.outstanding||0), accruedInterest:parseFloat(l.accrued_interest||0),
    interestRate:parseFloat(l.interest_rate||0), termMonths:l.term_months||0,
    repaymentFreq:l.repayment_freq||'Monthly', disbursedAt:l.disbursed_at,
    maturityDate:l.maturity_date, nextDue:l.next_due, debitAccountId:l.debit_account_id,
    debitAccountNumber:l.debit_account_number||'', source:l.source||'', productType:l.product_type||'',
    repaymentMode:l.repayment_mode||'SCHEDULED', repaymentAmount:parseFloat(l.repayment_amount||0), interestIncluded: l.interest_included !== 0,
    repaymentPct:parseFloat(l.repayment_pct||0), autoDeduct:l.auto_deduct!==0,
    loanModule: l.loan_module || 'BANK', insuranceFee: parseFloat(l.insurance_fee || 0)
  }));
  // Build loan schedule from each loan's embedded schedule data
  DB.loanSchedule = [];
  if (results[6].status === 'fulfilled' && results[6].value?.data) {
    results[6].value.data.forEach(l => {
      if (l.schedule && Array.isArray(l.schedule)) {
        l.schedule.forEach(s => DB.loanSchedule.push({
          id: s.id, loanId: l.id, installment: s.installment, due: s.due_date||s.due,
          principal: parseFloat(s.principal||0), interest: parseFloat(s.interest||0),
          // ★ FIX (DASH-003): Avoid double-counting interest when total_amount already includes it
          total: s.total_amount ? parseFloat(s.total_amount) : parseFloat(s.principal||0) + parseFloat(s.interest||0),
          paid: parseFloat(s.paid||0), status: s.status||'DUE'
        }));
      }
    });
  }
  // ★ FIXED: Build loanApplications from the real loan_applications table via API.
  // Previously derived from DB.loans by filtering PENDING/APPROVED/UNDER_REVIEW status,
  // which missed the 4 actual applications in the dedicated loan_applications table.
  // Now populated from result[22] (API.get('loan-applications')) with real DB checks.
  // Fallback: if the API call failed, still derive from loans for backward compatibility.
  DB.loanApplications = DB.loans.filter(l => l.status === 'PENDING' || l.status === 'APPROVED' || l.status === 'UNDER_REVIEW').map(l => ({
    id: l.id, ref: l.loanNumber, customerId: l.customerId, customerName: l.customerName,
    amount: l.principal, term: l.termMonths, purpose: l.source || '',
    status: l.status, branch: l.branch, appliedAt: l.disbursedAt || new Date().toISOString(),
    interestRate: l.interestRate, debitAccountId: l.debitAccountId,
    debitAccountNumber: l.debitAccountNumber, disbursementAccountId: null,
    repaymentMode: l.repaymentMode, repaymentAmount: l.repaymentAmount,
    repaymentPct: l.repaymentPct, autoDeduct: l.autoDeduct,
    _dbId: l.id,
    checks: [
      {code:'KYC',name:'KYC Verification',status:(l.status==='UNDER_REVIEW'||l.status==='APPROVED')?'PASSED':'PENDING'},
      {code:'CREDIT',name:'Credit Bureau Check',status:(l.status==='UNDER_REVIEW'||l.status==='APPROVED')?'PASSED':'PENDING'},
      {code:'HOLDING',name:'Minimum Holding Rule (120 days)',status:(l.status==='UNDER_REVIEW'||l.status==='APPROVED')?'PASSED':'PENDING'},
      {code:'AFFORDABILITY',name:'Affordability Assessment',status:(l.status==='UNDER_REVIEW'||l.status==='APPROVED')?'PASSED':'PENDING'}
    ]
  }));
  // ★ Deduplicate: if an approval already exists for a loan application, sync status to UNDER_REVIEW
  // This handles edge cases where the backend PUT failed but the approval was created
  // Also handles ID mismatches: approval may use backend ID while application uses local ID (or vice versa)
  DB.loanApplications.forEach(la => {
    if (la.status !== 'PENDING') return;
    const existingApproval = DB.approvals.find(a =>
      a.entityType === 'Loan Application' && a.status === 'PENDING' &&
      (a.entityId === la.id || a.entityId === la._dbId));
    if (!existingApproval) {
      // Ultimate fallback: match by customer + amount (covers legacy approvals with mismatched IDs)
      const fallbackApproval = DB.approvals.find(a =>
        a.entityType === 'Loan Application' && a.status === 'PENDING' &&
        a.value && a.value.includes(la.customerName) && a.value.includes(String(la.amount)));
      if (fallbackApproval) {
        la.status = 'UNDER_REVIEW';
        la.checks.forEach(ch => ch.status = 'PASSED');
        const dbLoan = DB.loans.find(l => l.id === la._dbId);
        if (dbLoan) dbLoan.status = 'UNDER_REVIEW';
        return;
      }
      return;
    }
    la.status = 'UNDER_REVIEW';
    la.checks.forEach(ch => ch.status = 'PASSED');
    const dbLoan = DB.loans.find(l => l.id === la._dbId);
    if (dbLoan) dbLoan.status = 'UNDER_REVIEW';
  });
  if (results[7].status === 'fulfilled' && results[7].value?.data) DB.settings = results[7].value.data.map(s => ({
    id:s.id, key:s.key, name:s.name, category:s.category, value:s.value,
    description:s.description||'', effectiveFrom:s.effective_from, requiresApproval:!!s.requires_approval
  }));
  // ★ [8] chart-of-accounts: load basic account list (WITHOUT balances).
  // NOTE: results[20] (general-ledger) OVERWRITES this with balances included.
  // We load [8] first as a fallback in case [20] fails — but renderGLAccounts()
  // will show a warning if netBalance data is missing (indicating API [20] failed).
  if (results[8].status === 'fulfilled' && results[8].value?.data) DB.chartOfAccounts = results[8].value.data.map(c => ({
    code:c.code, name:c.name, type:c.type, category:c.category||'', description:c.description||'', isActive:!!c.is_active
  }));
  if (results[9].status === 'fulfilled' && results[9].value?.data) DB.approvals = results[9].value.data.map(a => {
    let parsedDetails = null;
    if (a.details) {
      try { parsedDetails = JSON.parse(a.details); } catch(e) { parsedDetails = null; }
    }
    // ★ FIX (APPR-002/003): Backend now JOINs staff table to provide submitted_by_name and decided_by_name.
    // Fall back to raw ID if the JOIN columns are missing (e.g. staff record deleted).
    const submittedByName = a.submitted_by_name || (a.submitted_by ? String(a.submitted_by) : '');
    const decidedByName = a.decided_by_name || (a.decided_by ? String(a.decided_by) : '');
    // ★ FIX (APPR-010): Match BOTH 'TXN_REVERSE' (frontend scope) and 'txn.reverse' (legacy scope)
    // for _reversalDetails — previously only 'txn.reverse' was matched, causing null details
    // for newly-created reversal approvals that use 'TXN_REVERSE'.
    const isReversalScope = a.scope_code==='txn.reverse' || a.scope_code==='TXN_REVERSE';
    return {
      id:a.id, entityType:a.entity_type, entityId:a.entity_id, scopeCode:a.scope_code,
      status:a.status, submittedBy:submittedByName, branch:a.branch||'',
      value:a.value||'', submittedAt:a.submitted_at, decidedBy:decidedByName,
      decidedAt:a.decided_at, reason:a.reason||'',
      _reversalDetails: isReversalScope ? parsedDetails : null,
      _writeoffDetails: a.scope_code==='loans.writeoff' ? parsedDetails : null,
      _settingDetails: a.scope_code==='settings.manage' ? parsedDetails : null
    };
  });
  if (results[10].status === 'fulfilled' && results[10].value?.data) DB.auditLogs = results[10].value.data.map(a => ({
    id:a.id, uuid:a.uuid, actor:a.actor, actorBranch:a.actor_branch, action:a.action,
    entity:a.entity, entityId:a.entity_id, result:a.result, ip:a.ip, details:a.details||'', timestamp:a.timestamp,
    module:a.module, category:a.category, userAgent:a.user_agent
  }));
  if (results[11].status === 'fulfilled' && results[11].value?.data) DB.notifications = results[11].value.data.map(n => ({
    id:n.id, type:n.type||'INFO', title:n.title, body:n.body||n.message||'', status:n.status||'PENDING',
    channel:n.channel||'IN_APP', timestamp:n.timestamp, priority:n.priority||'NORMAL',
    entityType:n.entity_type||'', entityId:n.entity_id||'', actor:n.actor||''
  }));
  if (results[12].status === 'fulfilled' && results[12].value?.data) DB.policies = results[12].value.data.map(p => ({
    id:p.id, code:p.code, version:p.version, name:p.name, effective_from:p.effective_from,
    effective_to:p.effective_to, description:p.description||'', content:p.content||'',
    category:p.category, status:p.status, severity:p.severity,
    owner:p.owner, review_cycle_days:p.review_cycle_days,
    next_review_date:p.next_review_date, last_reviewed_at:p.last_reviewed_at,
    revision_count:p.revision_count||0, is_expired:p.is_expired||false,
    is_expiring_soon:p.is_expiring_soon||false, review_overdue:p.review_overdue||false
  }));
  if (results[13].status === 'fulfilled' && results[13].value?.data) DB.expenses = results[13].value.data.map(e => ({
    id:e.id, date:e.date, category:e.category, glCode:e.gl_code, glAccountName:e.gl_account_name,
    amount:parseFloat(e.amount||0), vendor:e.vendor||'', description:e.description||'',
    notes:e.notes||'', branch:e.branch||'', status:e.status,
    approvedBy:e.approved_by_name||e.approved_by||'', approvedAt:e.approved_at,
    operator:e.operator_name||'', timestamp:e.created_at
  }));
  // [14] Documents — map DB snake_case columns to frontend camelCase fields
  if (results[14].status === 'fulfilled' && results[14].value?.data) {
    const typeMap = { STMT: 'STATEMENT', PAY: 'PAYSLIP', RCPT: 'RECEIPT', REPORT: 'REPORT' };
    const statusMap = { FINAL: 'ACTIVE', DRAFT: 'ACTIVE', CANCELLED: 'VOIDED' };
    DB.generatedDocuments = results[14].value.data.map(d => ({
      id: d.id,
      documentNumber: d.document_number,
      type: typeMap[d.type] || d.type,
      subtype: d.subtype || '',
      accountNumber: d.account_number || '',
      customerId: d.customer_id || 0,
      customerName: d.customer_name || '',
      branch: d.branch || '',
      periodFrom: d.period_start || '',
      periodTo: d.period_end || '',
      operator: d.generated_by_name || '',
      generatedAt: d.created_at || '',
      printCount: d.print_count || 0,
      lastPrintedAt: d.last_printed_at || null,
      exportCount: d.export_count || 0,
      lastExportedAt: d.last_exported_at || null,
      summary: d.summary || null,
      status: statusMap[d.status] || d.status || 'ACTIVE'
    }));
  }
  
  // [15] Dashboard report data — store for renderDashboard to use
  // ★ FIX (DP-011): Previously fetched but completely discarded. The backend dashboard
  // provides server-computed, branch-filtered counts that should be the source of truth.
  // Now stored in STATE.dashServerData for the dashboard KPI cards to consume.
  if (results[15].status === 'fulfilled' && results[15].value?.data) {
    STATE.dashServerData = results[15].value.data;
  }

  // [16] Balance trends — populate chart data from database
  if (results[16].status === 'fulfilled' && results[16].value?.data) {
    const trends = results[16].value.data;
    if (Array.isArray(trends) && trends.length > 0) {
      const labels = [...new Set(trends.map(t => t.snapshot_date))].sort();
      const productTypes = [...new Set(trends.map(t => t.product_type))];
      DB.balanceTrends = { labels };
      productTypes.forEach(pt => {
        DB.balanceTrends[pt.charAt(0) + pt.slice(1).toLowerCase()] =
          labels.map(label => {
            const match = trends.find(t => t.snapshot_date === label && t.product_type === pt);
            return match ? parseFloat(match.total_balance || 0) : 0;
          });
      });
    }
  }

  // [17] Profit ledger — load from database
  if (results[17].status === 'fulfilled' && results[17].value?.data) {
    DB.profitLedger = results[17].value.data.map(e => ({
      id: e.id, glAccount: e.gl_code||e.gl_account, glAccountName: e.gl_account_name||e.gl_name,
      // ★ FIX (DP-001): Map gl_type field so the dashboard fallback can filter income vs expense.
      // Previously, the dashboard fallback used e.direction!=='debit' but 'direction' doesn't
      // exist in profit_ledger — only gl_type (INCOME/EXPENSE). This caused ALL entries to pass
      // the filter, potentially including expense-type entries in the income total.
      glType: e.gl_type||'INCOME',
      category: e.category, sourceRef: e.source_ref||'', sourceType: e.source_type||'',
      accountNumber: e.account_number||'', accountType: e.account_type||'',
      customerName: e.customer_name||'', branch: e.branch||'',
      grossAmount: parseFloat(e.gross_amount||e.amount||0),
      // ★ FIX (RA-CF-003): Use nullish-coalescing check instead of || for feeAmount.
      // When e.fee_amount is 0 (a legitimate value), || treats it as falsy and falls through
      // to e.amount (gross amount), inflating feeAmount. Now 0 is preserved as a valid value.
      feeAmount: e.fee_amount != null && e.fee_amount !== '' ? parseFloat(e.fee_amount) : parseFloat(e.amount||0),
      feePct: parseFloat(e.fee_pct||0), feeMode: e.fee_mode||'',
      operator: e.operator||'', description: e.description||'',
      timestamp: e.created_at||e.timestamp||new Date().toISOString(),
      date: (e.created_at||e.timestamp||'').slice(0,10)
    }));
  }

  // [18] Operating account — load from database
  if (results[18].status === 'fulfilled' && results[18].value?.data) {
    const op = results[18].value.data;
    // ★ FIX: Specifically find BANK-OP-0001 from the array — NOT blindly op[0]
    // If OPS-000001 and BANK-OP-0001 both exist, op[0] would pick the wrong one.
    const src = Array.isArray(op) && op.length > 0
      ? (op.find(a => a.account_number === 'BANK-OP-0001') || op[0])
      : (op && typeof op === 'object' ? op : null);
    if (src) {
      // Normalize transactions from API — amounts are strings from MySQL DECIMAL
      // ★ FIX (OPFUND-001): Map branch, contraAccount, transactionType consistently
      // with refreshGLData() to ensure branch filtering works on initial load.
      const txns = (src.transactions || []).map(t => ({
        ...t,
        id: t.id,
        ref: t.ref || '',
        date: t.date || '',
        type: t.type || '',
        description: t.description || '',
        amount: parseFloat(t.amount) || 0,
        balance: parseFloat(t.balance_after || t.balance) || 0,
        operator: t.operator || '',
        branch: t.branch || '',
        contraAccount: t.contra_account || '',
        transactionType: t.transaction_type || '',
        timestamp: t.created_at || ''
      }));
      DB.operatingAccount = {
        accountNumber: src.account_number||'BANK-OP-0001',
        accountName: src.account_name||'Atlas Bank Operating Fund',
        balance: parseFloat(src.balance||src.ledger_balance||0),
        currency: src.currency||'XAF',
        transactions: txns
      };
    }
  }

  // [19] Loan fund accounts — load from database (BANK-LF-0001 and BANK-LI-0001)
  if (results[19].status === 'fulfilled' && results[19].value?.data) {
    const fundAccounts = results[19].value.data;
    if (Array.isArray(fundAccounts)) {
      fundAccounts.forEach(acct => {
        const mappedTxns = (acct.transactions||[]).map(t => ({
          id: t.id, ref: t.ref, date: t.date, type: t.type,
          description: t.description, amount: parseFloat(t.amount||0),
          balance: parseFloat(t.balance_after||0),
          operator: t.operator||'', timestamp: t.created_at||''
        }));
        if (acct.fundType === 'LOAN_FUND' || acct.accountNumber === 'BANK-LF-0001') {
          DB.loanFundAccount = {
            accountNumber: acct.accountNumber||'BANK-LF-0001',
            accountName: acct.accountName||'Loans & Advances Fund',
            balance: parseFloat(acct.balance||0),
            currency: acct.currency||'XAF',
            transactions: mappedTxns
          };
        } else if (acct.fundType === 'LOAN_INTEREST' || acct.accountNumber === 'BANK-LI-0001') {
          DB.loanInterestAccount = {
            accountNumber: acct.accountNumber||'BANK-LI-0001',
            accountName: acct.accountName||'Loan Interest Income',
            balance: parseFloat(acct.balance||0),
            currency: acct.currency||'XAF',
            transactions: mappedTxns
          };
        }
      });
    }
  }

  // [20] General Ledger — accounts with computed balances + trial balance
  if (results[20] && results[20].status === 'fulfilled' && results[20].value?.data) {
    const glData = results[20].value.data;
    if (glData && !Array.isArray(glData) && glData.accounts) {
      // Use GL accounts data (includes computed net_balance, entry_count, etc.)
      DB.chartOfAccounts = glData.accounts.map(a => ({
        code: a.code, name: a.name, type: a.type, category: a.category||'',
        description: a.description||'', isActive: !!a.is_active,
        totalDebit: parseFloat(a.total_debit)||0, totalCredit: parseFloat(a.total_credit)||0,
        netBalance: parseFloat(a.net_balance)||0, entryCount: a.entry_count||0,
        lastEntryDate: a.last_entry_date||''
      }));
      // Store trial balance
      DB.trialBalance = glData.trial_balance || {};
    }
  }
  // [21] General Ledger journal entries (paginated)
  if (results[21] && results[21].status === 'fulfilled' && results[21].value?.data) {
    const glEntries = results[21].value.data;
    if (Array.isArray(glEntries)) {
      DB.generalLedger = glEntries.map(e => ({
        id: e.id, date: e.date||'', reference: e.reference||'',
        accountCode: e.account_code||'', accountName: e.account_name||'',
        debit: parseFloat(e.debit)||0, credit: parseFloat(e.credit)||0,
        contraAccount: e.contra_account||'', transactionType: e.transaction_type||'',
        description: e.description||'', postedBy: e.posted_by_name||'',
        branch: e.branch||''
      }));
    }
  }

  // ★ [22] Real loan applications from the dedicated loan_applications table.
  // This overrides the fallback derivation from DB.loans above.
  // Each application comes with its real due diligence checks from loan_application_checks.
  if (results[22] && results[22].status === 'fulfilled' && results[22].value?.data) {
    const appData = results[22].value.data;
    if (Array.isArray(appData) && appData.length > 0) {
      DB.loanApplications = appData.map(a => ({
        id: a.id,
        ref: a.ref || ('LA-' + a.id),
        customerId: a.customer_id,
        customerName: a.customer_name || '',
        amount: parseFloat(a.amount) || 0,
        term: parseInt(a.term) || 0,
        purpose: a.purpose || '',
        status: a.status || 'PENDING',
        branch: a.branch || '',
        appliedAt: a.applied_at || '',
        interestRate: parseFloat(a.interest_rate) || 0,
        productType: a.product_type || '',
        repaymentMode: a.repayment_mode || 'SCHEDULED',
        debitAccountId: a.debit_account_id || null,
        debitAccountNumber: a.debit_account_number || '',
        disbursementAccountId: a.disbursement_account_id ? Number(a.disbursement_account_id) : null,
        repaymentAmount: 0,
        repaymentPct: 0,
        autoDeduct: true,
        _dbId: a.loan_id || a.id, // ★ loan_id links to loans table; fallback to application id
        _appId: a.id, // ★ Always preserve the real application ID
        _isRealApplication: true, // ★ Flag: this came from loan_applications table, not loans table
        _decisionReason: a.decision_reason || '',
        _decidedBy: a.decided_by,
        _decidedAt: a.decided_at,
        // ★ Real due diligence checks from loan_application_checks table
        checks: Array.isArray(a.checks) ? a.checks.map(ch => ({
          code: ch.code || '',
          name: ch.name || ch.code || '',
          status: ch.status || 'PENDING'
        })) : [
          {code:'KYC_CHECK',name:'KYC Verification',status:'PENDING'},
          {code:'CREDIT_SCORE',name:'Credit Bureau Check',status:'PENDING'},
          {code:'INCOME_VERIFY',name:'Income Verification',status:'PENDING'},
          {code:'COLLATERAL_CHECK',name:'Collateral Assessment',status:'PENDING'}
        ]
      }));
      // Also merge any PENDING/APPROVED/UNDER_REVIEW loans from DB.loans that aren't already
      // represented (covers loans created directly via POST /api/loans without a formal application)
      const realAppCustomerIds = new Set(DB.loanApplications.filter(la => la._isRealApplication).map(la => la.customerId));
      const loansDerivedApps = DB.loans.filter(l =>
        (l.status === 'PENDING' || l.status === 'APPROVED' || l.status === 'UNDER_REVIEW') &&
        !realAppCustomerIds.has(l.customerId)
      ).map(l => ({
        id: l.id, ref: l.loanNumber, customerId: l.customerId, customerName: l.customerName,
        amount: l.principal, term: l.termMonths, purpose: l.source || '',
        status: l.status, branch: l.branch, appliedAt: l.disbursedAt || new Date().toISOString(),
        interestRate: l.interestRate, debitAccountId: l.debitAccountId,
        debitAccountNumber: l.debitAccountNumber, disbursementAccountId: null,
        repaymentMode: l.repaymentMode, repaymentAmount: l.repaymentAmount,
        repaymentPct: l.repaymentPct, autoDeduct: l.autoDeduct,
        _dbId: l.id, _isRealApplication: false,
        checks: [
          {code:'KYC',name:'KYC Verification',status:(l.status==='UNDER_REVIEW'||l.status==='APPROVED')?'PASSED':'PENDING'},
          {code:'CREDIT',name:'Credit Bureau Check',status:(l.status==='UNDER_REVIEW'||l.status==='APPROVED')?'PASSED':'PENDING'},
          {code:'HOLDING',name:'Minimum Holding Rule (120 days)',status:(l.status==='UNDER_REVIEW'||l.status==='APPROVED')?'PASSED':'PENDING'},
          {code:'AFFORDABILITY',name:'Affordability Assessment',status:(l.status==='UNDER_REVIEW'||l.status==='APPROVED')?'PASSED':'PENDING'}
        ]
      }));
      DB.loanApplications = [...DB.loanApplications, ...loansDerivedApps];
    }
  }

  // Load audit findings from database (using audit/findings endpoint) — only if user has AUDIT module
  if (_has('AUDIT')) {
    const findingsRes = await API.get('audit/findings', {pageSize:500});
    if (findingsRes?.data) {
      DB.auditFindings = findingsRes.data.map(f => ({
        id:f.id, severity:f.severity, category:f.category, description:f.description||'',
        branch:f.branch||'', assignee:f.assignee||'', status:f.status||'OPEN',
        timestamp:f.timestamp||f.created_at, actions:f.remediation_actions||''
      }));
      // Sync to STATE for audit panel
      STATE.auditFindings = findingsRes.data;
    }
    // Load login history for Audit Trail → Login History tab (database driven)
    const loginHistoryRes = await API.get('auth/login-history', {limit: 200});
    if (loginHistoryRes?.data && Array.isArray(loginHistoryRes.data)) {
      DB.loginHistory = loginHistoryRes.data.map(l => ({
        id: l.id, user: l.username||'', result: l.result||'', ip: l.ip||'',
        ua: l.user_agent||'', risk: l.risk||'NONE', timestamp: l.created_at||l.timestamp||''
      }));
    }
  }

  // ★ Update sidebar stats from live database data (not hardcoded)
  const sidebarBS = $('sidebarBranchScope');
  if (sidebarBS) {
    const branchCount = (STATE.currentUser?.role || '').toUpperCase() === 'ADMIN'
      ? (DB.branches?.length || 0)
      : (STATE.currentUser?.branches?.length || 0);
    sidebarBS.textContent = branchCount + (branchCount === 1 ? ' branch' : ' branches');
  }
  const sidebarPV = $('sidebarPolicyVersion');
  if (sidebarPV && DB.policies && DB.policies.length > 0) {
    const latest = [...DB.policies].sort((a, b) => (b.version || 0) - (a.version || 0))[0];
    sidebarPV.textContent = latest.code || '—';
  }
  const sidebarMC = $('sidebarMakerChecker');
  if (sidebarMC) {
    // Maker-checker is active when the approval policy exists in the database (no module gate)
    const mcPolicy = (DB.policies || []).find(p => (p.code || '').includes('APPROVAL'));
    sidebarMC.textContent = mcPolicy ? 'Active' : '—';
  }

  // ★ Sync current user's profile picture to header/dropdown from DB
  if (STATE.currentUser && DB.staff.length > 0) {
    const me = DB.staff.find(s => s.id === STATE.currentUser.id);
    if (me && me.profilePicture) {
      STATE.currentUser.profilePicture = me.profilePicture;
      setAvatarImage($('headerAvatar'), me.profilePicture, STATE.currentUser.initials);
      setAvatarImage($('udAvatar'), me.profilePicture, STATE.currentUser.initials);
    }
  }

  showToast('success','Synced','All data loaded from server.');

  // Populate all branch selectors with live database data
  populateAllBranchSelectors();
}


/* ================================================================
   DOCUMENT TRACKING HELPERS
   ================================================================ */
function generateDocumentNumber(type) {
  const prefixMap = { STMT: 'DOC-STMT', PAY: 'DOC-PAY', RCPT: 'DOC-RCPT' };
  const prefix = prefixMap[type] || 'DOC-GEN';
  const today = new Date().toISOString().slice(0,10).replace(/-/g,'');
  const existing = DB.generatedDocuments.filter(d => d.type === type && d.documentNumber.includes(today));
  const seq = String(existing.length + 1).padStart(4, '0');
  // Append milliseconds to guarantee uniqueness even across page refreshes
  const ms = String(Date.now() % 1000).padStart(3, '0');
  return prefix + '-' + today + '-' + seq + ms;
}

function registerDocument(record) {
  if (!record.id) record.id = DB.generatedDocuments.length + 1;
  // Check if we already have this document locally (re-print)
  const local = DB.generatedDocuments.find(d => d.documentNumber === record.documentNumber);
  if (local) {
    local.printCount = (local.printCount || 0) + 1;
    local.lastPrintedAt = new Date().toISOString();
    return local;
  }
  DB.generatedDocuments.push(record);
  // Persist to API — use silent fetch (no toast) since this is a background audit operation
  const dbTypeMap = { STATEMENT: 'STMT', PAYSLIP: 'PAY', RECEIPT: 'RCPT', REPORT: 'REPORT' };
  const payload = {
    document_number: record.documentNumber,
    type: dbTypeMap[record.type] || record.type || 'REPORT',
    subtype: record.subtype || '',
    account_number: record.accountNumber || '',
    account_type: record.accountType || '',
    customer_name: record.customerName || '',
    branch: record.branch || 'All Branches',
    period_start: record.periodFrom || record.periodStart || '',
    period_end: record.periodTo || record.periodEnd || '',
    generated_by_name: record.generatedByName || record.operator || '',
    summary: record.summary || null
  };
  fetch(API_BASE + 'documents', {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: {
      'Content-Type': 'application/json',
      ...(authToken ? { 'Authorization': 'Bearer ' + authToken } : {}),
      ...(csrfToken ? { 'X-CSRF-Token': csrfToken } : {})
    },
    credentials: 'include',
    body: JSON.stringify(payload)
  }).then(async resp => {
    if (resp.ok) {
      const data = await resp.json().catch(() => null);
      if (data?.data?.id) record.id = data.data.id;
    } else {
      console.warn('[Document] Backend registration returned ' + resp.status);
    }
  }).catch(err => {
    console.warn('[Document] Backend registration failed (may already exist):', err);
  });
  return record;
}

function findDocumentById(docId) {
  return DB.generatedDocuments.find(d => d.id === docId);
}

function findDocumentByNumber(docNumber) {
  return DB.generatedDocuments.find(d => d.documentNumber === docNumber);
}

const $ = id => document.getElementById(id);
const $$ = sel => document.querySelectorAll(sel);
// ★ FIX (DASH-009): Guard against NaN/undefined/null input to prevent "NaN FCFA" display
const money = (v) => new Intl.NumberFormat('fr-FR',{style:'decimal',minimumFractionDigits:0,maximumFractionDigits:0}).format((v != null && !isNaN(v)) ? v : 0) + ' FCFA';
const formatChartNum = (v) => { if (v == null || isNaN(v)) return '0'; const abs = Math.abs(v); const sign = v < 0 ? '-' : ''; if (abs >= 1e9) return sign + (abs / 1e9).toFixed(1) + 'B'; if (abs >= 1e6) return sign + (abs / 1e6).toFixed(1) + 'M'; if (abs >= 1e3) return sign + (abs / 1e3).toFixed(1) + 'K'; return sign + Math.round(abs).toString(); };
const dateFmt = (d) => new Date(d).toLocaleString('en-US',{year:'numeric',month:'short',day:'2-digit',hour:'2-digit',minute:'2-digit'});
const shortDate = (d) => new Date(d).toLocaleString('en-US',{month:'short',day:'2-digit',year:'numeric'});
const norm = v => String(v??'').toLowerCase();
/* Enterprise debounce utility — prevents excessive re-renders on fast typing */
const _debounceTimers = {};
function debounce(id, fn, ms) { ms = ms || 220; clearTimeout(_debounceTimers[id]); _debounceTimers[id] = setTimeout(fn, ms); }
/* Enterprise crypto-secure random — replaces Math.random() for all security contexts */
function _cryptoRandom(max) { const arr = new Uint32Array(1); crypto.getRandomValues(arr); return arr[0] % max; }
function _cryptoRandomHex(len) { const arr = new Uint8Array(len); crypto.getRandomValues(arr); return Array.from(arr, b => b.toString(16).padStart(2, '0')).join('').toUpperCase(); }
/* Safe search action dispatch — uses pre-registered function references, NEVER eval()/new Function() */
function _searchNavigateTo(panel, drawerFn, drawerId) {
  closeSearchOverlay();
  navigate(panel);
  if (drawerFn && drawerId != null) {
    setTimeout(() => { try { drawerFn(drawerId); } catch(e) { console.warn('[Search] Drawer open failed:', e); } }, 200);
  }
}
function dispatchSearchAction(actionStr) {
  /* Primary: use the pre-registered function from STATE._actionFns (set during executeSearch) */
  const idx = STATE._flatResults?.[STATE.searchActiveIndex]?.__fnIdx;
  if (idx !== undefined && STATE._actionFns?.[idx]) { STATE._actionFns[idx](); return; }
  /* Fallback: dispatch table for known navigation actions (no new Function/eval) */
  if (typeof actionStr === 'function') { actionStr(); return; }
  if (!actionStr || typeof actionStr !== 'string') return;
  const navMap = {
    'navigate(customers)': () => { closeSearchOverlay(); navigate('customers'); },
    'navigate(accounts)': () => { closeSearchOverlay(); navigate('accounts'); },
    'navigate(transactions)': () => { closeSearchOverlay(); navigate('transactions'); },
    'navigate(loans)': () => { closeSearchOverlay(); navigate('loans'); },
    'navigate(staff)': () => { closeSearchOverlay(); navigate('staff'); },
    'navigate(settings)': () => { closeSearchOverlay(); navigate('settings'); },
    'navigate(audit)': () => { closeSearchOverlay(); navigate('audit'); },
    'navigate(expenses)': () => { closeSearchOverlay(); navigate('expenses'); },
    'navigate(documents)': () => { closeSearchOverlay(); navigate('documents'); },
    'navigate(policies)': () => { closeSearchOverlay(); navigate('settings'); },
    'closeSearchOverlay()': () => closeSearchOverlay(),
  };
  const stripped = actionStr.replace(/\s/g, '');
  for (const [key, fn] of Object.entries(navMap)) {
    if (stripped.includes(key.replace(/\s/g, ''))) { fn(); return; }
  }
}
const pillCls = v => ({PENDING:'pill-pending',PENDING_APPROVAL:'pill-pending',APPROVED:'pill-approved',POSTED:'pill-posted',ACTIVE:'pill-active',REJECTED:'pill-rejected',FAILED:'pill-failed',DENIED:'pill-denied',DELINQUENT:'pill-delinquent',RESTRICTED:'pill-restricted',PENDING_KYC:'pill-pending',UNDER_REVIEW:'pill-review',DRAFT:'pill-pending',CLOSED:'pill-rejected',REVERSED:'pill-warning',CANCELLED:'pill-rejected',ABSORBED:'pill-absorbed',MISSED:'pill-delinquency',PARTIALLY_PAID:'pill-review',WAIVED:'pill-warning',DUE:'pill-pending',LOW:'pill-low',MEDIUM:'pill-medium',HIGH:'pill-high',SUCCESS:'pill-approved',FAILURE:'pill-rejected',LOCKED:'pill-delinquency',SALARY:'pill-salary',CURRENT:'pill-current',SAVINGS:'pill-savings',FROZEN:'pill-frozen',DORMANT:'pill-dormant',PENDING_OPENING:'pill-pending',SETTLED:'pill-approved',DISBURSED:'pill-active',WRITTEN_OFF:'pill-failed',DEFAULTED:'pill-delinquency',RESTRUCTURED:'pill-review',IN_PROGRESS:'pill-review',RESOLVED:'pill-approved',ESCALATED:'pill-danger',INACTIVE:'pill-dormant',SUSPENDED:'pill-warning',OPEN:'pill-pending'})[v]||'pill-pending';

function paginate(arr, page, size) {
  const start = (page - 1) * size;
  const totalPages = Math.ceil(arr.length / size) || 1;
  return { items: arr.slice(start, start + size), total: arr.length, pages: totalPages, totalPages: totalPages };
}
function renderPagination(containerId, current, total, onPageChange, pageSize) {
  const c = $(containerId); if (!c) return;
  const ps = pageSize || STATE.txnPageSize;
  const pages = Math.ceil(total / ps) || 1;
  // ★ FIX: onPageChange can be a string (function name) or a function reference.
  // If it's a function, we generate a unique global wrapper so the onclick handler works.
  let handlerName;
  if (typeof onPageChange === 'function') {
    handlerName = '__pg_' + containerId;
    window[handlerName] = onPageChange;
  } else {
    handlerName = onPageChange;
  }
  c.innerHTML = `<div class="pagination-info">Showing ${total===0?0:((current-1)*ps)+1}–${Math.min(current*ps,total)} of ${total}</div><div class="pagination-btns">${Array.from({length:pages},(_,i)=>`<button ${i+1===current?'class="active':''} onclick="${handlerName}(${i+1})">${i+1}</button>`).join('')}</div>`;
}

/* ================================================================
   TOAST NOTIFICATION SYSTEM
   ================================================================ */
function showToast(type, title, msg, duration = 4000) {
  const toastIcons = {success:'check-circle',error:'x-circle',warning:'alert-triangle',info:'info'};
  const t = document.createElement('div');
  t.className = `toast toast-${type}`;
  t.innerHTML = `<span class="toast-icon">${IC(toastIcons[type],20)}</span><div class="toast-body"><div class="toast-title">${title}</div>${msg?`<div class="toast-msg">${msg}</div>`:''}</div><button class="toast-close" onclick="this.parentElement.remove()">${IC('x',16)}</button>`;
  $('toastContainer').appendChild(t);
  setTimeout(()=>{t.style.animation='slideOut .3s ease forwards';setTimeout(()=>t.remove(),300)},duration);
}

/* ================================================================
   MODAL SYSTEM
   ================================================================ */
function openModal(id) { $(id).classList.add('open'); }
function closeModal(id) { $(id).classList.remove('open'); }
function showConfirm(title, msg, action, requireReason = false, isHTML = false) {
  $('confirmTitle').textContent = title;
  if (isHTML) {
    $('confirmMessage').innerHTML = msg;
    refreshIcons($('confirmMessage'));
  } else {
    $('confirmMessage').textContent = msg;
  }
  $('confirmReasonGroup').style.display = requireReason ? 'grid' : 'none';
  $('confirmActionBtn').textContent = 'Confirm';
  STATE.pendingConfirmAction = action;
  openModal('confirmModal');
}
function closeConfirm() { closeModal('confirmModal'); STATE.pendingConfirmAction = null; }
function executeConfirmAction() {
  const reason = $('confirmReason').value;
  if (STATE.pendingConfirmAction) STATE.pendingConfirmAction(reason);
  closeConfirm();
  $('confirmReason').value = '';
}

}

}

/* ================================================================
   AUTH SYSTEM
   ================================================================ */
function handleLogin(e) {
  e.preventDefault();
  const user = $('loginUser').value.trim();
  const pass = $('loginPass').value;
  $('loginUserError').classList.remove('show');
  $('loginPassError').classList.remove('show');
  $('loginGeneralError').classList.remove('show');
  if (!user) { $('loginUserError').textContent='Username is required'; $('loginUserError').classList.add('show'); return false; }
  if (!pass) { $('loginPassError').textContent='Password is required'; $('loginPassError').classList.add('show'); return false; }
  $('loginBtn').disabled = true;
  $('loginBtn').textContent = 'Authenticating...';
  Loader.setStage('Authenticating credentials');

  // Try API authentication first
  API.post('auth', { username: user, password: pass }).then(res => {
    $('loginBtn').disabled = false;
    $('loginBtn').textContent = 'Sign In';
    if (res && res.data) {
      authToken = res.data.token;
      csrfToken = res.data.csrf_token || '';
      localStorage.setItem('atlas_token', authToken);
      const apiUser = {
        id: res.data.staff_id, username: res.data.username, fullName: res.data.full_name,
        initials: res.data.initials, role: res.data.role, department: res.data.department,
        email: res.data.email, phone: res.data.phone, position: res.data.position,
        branches: res.data.branches, modules: res.data.modules,
        approvalLimit: res.data.approval_limit, mfaRequired: !!res.data.mfa_required,
        employmentStatus: 'ACTIVE', profilePicture: res.data.profile_picture || ''
      };
      // Apply DB-configured session timeout (in minutes) from backend
      if (res.data.session_timeout) {
        STATE.sessionTimeout = parseInt(res.data.session_timeout) * 60;
      }
      // MFA gate: if backend requires MFA, show modal and wait for server-side verification
      // New device notification
      if (res.data.new_device) {
        showToast('warning', 'New Device Detected', 'Login from ' + (res.data.device_label || 'unrecognized device') + '. If this was not you, change your password immediately.');
      }
      // MFA gate: if backend requires MFA, show modal and wait for server-side verification
      if (res.data.mfa_required && !res.data.mfa_verified) {
        STATE.currentUser = apiUser;
        STATE._pendingMfa = true;
        STATE._forcePasswordChange = !!res.data.requires_password_change;
        $('loginScreen').style.display = 'none';
        openModal('mfaModal');
        setTimeout(() => $('mfaCode')?.focus(), 150);
        return;
      }
      // Force password change gate: redirect to profile password change
      if (res.data.requires_password_change) {
        STATE._forcePasswordChange = true;
        STATE._passwordChangeModalShown = true; // ★ FIX: Set guard immediately to prevent API wrapper from re-opening
        if (STATE._pendingMfa) { completeLogin(STATE._pendingMfa); STATE._pendingMfa = null; }
        else { completeLogin(apiUser); }
        // After completeLogin, open profile modal on Security tab for password change.
        // ★ FIX: The guard flag prevents any 403 from loadAllData() background calls
        // from re-triggering this modal.
        setTimeout(() => {
          openProfileModal('security');
          showToast('warning', 'Password Change Required', 'Your administrator requires you to change your password. Please update it now.');
        }, 500);
        return;
      }
      if (STATE._pendingMfa) { completeLogin(STATE._pendingMfa); STATE._pendingMfa = null; }
      else { completeLogin(apiUser); }
    } else {
      // API failed or returned error — show error
      $('loginGeneralError').textContent = 'Invalid credentials. Contact your administrator.';
      $('loginGeneralError').classList.add('show');
      addAuditEntry('LOGIN_FAILED','Session',user,'Failed login attempt for user "'+user+'".');
    }
  }).catch(() => {
    $('loginBtn').disabled = false;
    $('loginBtn').textContent = 'Sign In';
    $('loginGeneralError').textContent = 'Connection error. Please try again.';
    $('loginGeneralError').classList.add('show');
  });
  return false;
}

async function verifyMfa() {
  const code = $('mfaCode').value.trim();
  if (code.length !== 6 || !/\d{6}/.test(code)) { $('mfaError').textContent='Enter a valid 6-digit code'; $('mfaError').classList.add('show'); return; }
  $('mfaError').classList.remove('show');
  $('mfaVerifyBtn').disabled = true;
  $('mfaVerifyBtn').textContent = 'Verifying...';
  try {
    await refreshCsrfToken();
    const res = await API.post('auth/verify-mfa', { code: code });
    if (res) {
      csrfToken = res.data?.csrf_token || csrfToken;
      closeModal('mfaModal');
      $('mfaCode').value = '';
      STATE._pendingMfa = false;
      // ★ FIX: If force_password_change was deferred during MFA gate, set the guard
      // and open the password change modal after completeLogin.
      if (STATE._forcePasswordChange) {
        STATE._passwordChangeModalShown = true;
      }
      completeLogin(STATE.currentUser);
      addAuditEntry('MFA_VERIFIED','Session','MFA','Multi-factor authentication verified successfully.');
      // Open password change modal if deferred (MFA + force_password_change combo)
      if (STATE._forcePasswordChange) {
        setTimeout(() => {
          openProfileModal('security');
          showToast('warning', 'Password Change Required', 'Your administrator requires you to change your password. Please update it now.');
        }, 500);
      }
    } else {
      $('mfaError').textContent='Invalid MFA code. Please try again.';
      $('mfaError').classList.add('show');
    }
  } catch(e) {
    $('mfaError').textContent='Verification failed. Please try again.';
    $('mfaError').classList.add('show');
  }
  $('mfaVerifyBtn').disabled = false;
  $('mfaVerifyBtn').textContent = 'Verify';
}

function closeMfa() { closeModal('mfaModal'); $('mfaCode').value = ''; STATE.currentUser = null; }

async function completeLogin(user) {
  STATE.currentUser = user;
  $('loginScreen').style.display = 'none';
  // ★ FIX: Do NOT show appShell yet. Keep it hidden while we load data, render panels,
  // apply RBAC, and navigate to the correct view. This prevents the "flash" of the
  // default dashboard showing before the saved/restored view appears on page refresh.
  $('headerName').textContent = user.fullName;
  $('headerRole').textContent = user.role;
  setAvatarImage($('headerAvatar'), user.profilePicture, user.initials);
  $('udName').textContent = user.fullName;
  $('udRole').textContent = user.role + ' · ' + user.department;
  setAvatarImage($('udAvatar'), user.profilePicture, user.initials);
  startSessionTimer();
  addAuditEntry('LOGIN_SUCCESS','Session',user.username,'User '+user.fullName+' ('+user.role+') logged in successfully.');
  // Branch isolation: set initial branch from user's assigned branches
  const userBr = user.branches || [];
  if ((user.role || '').toUpperCase() === 'ADMIN') {
    // Admin defaults to 'All Branches' (company-wide view)
    STATE.currentBranch = ALL_BRANCHES;
    STATE.branchIndex = 0;
    STATE.branches = [ALL_BRANCHES]; // Will be populated in populateAllBranchSelectors
  } else if (userBr.length > 1) {
    // Multi-branch non-admin defaults to 'All My Branches' (all assigned)
    STATE.currentBranch = ALL_MY_BRANCHES;
    STATE.branchIndex = 0;
    STATE.branches = userBr;
  } else if (userBr.length === 1) {
    STATE.currentBranch = userBr[0];
    STATE.branchIndex = 0;
    STATE.branches = userBr;
  }
  // Load ALL data from the API backend before rendering
  Loader.setStage('Syncing enterprise modules');
  Loader.setProgress(50);
  await loadAllData();
  Loader.setStage('Building interface');
  Loader.setProgress(75);
  initApp();          // renders all panels + navigates to saved view (line ~9700)
  applyRoleAccess();  // hides restricted nav links
  Loader.setProgress(90);
  if (!STATE._restoring) showToast('success','Welcome back',`${user.fullName} signed in from ${user.branches?.[0] || 'system'}`);

  // ★ FIX: Skip background mutation engines when user has force_password_change.
  if (!STATE._forcePasswordChange) {
    await processAutoRepayments();
    // ★ CAPITALIZATION: Apply interest capitalization for any MISSED installments that
    // were missed in previous sessions. This ensures missed interest compounds into
    // principal even across page refreshes. The capitalizeMissedInterest() function
    // has a built-in idempotency guard (inst.interestCapitalized flag) so it only
    // runs once per installment.
    const delinquentLoans = DB.loans.filter(l => l.status === 'ACTIVE' || l.status === 'DELINQUENT');
    for (const loan of delinquentLoans) {
      await runCapitalizationForLoan(loan);
    }
    startAutoDeductionEngine();
    startNotificationPolling();
  }
  renderNotifications();

  // ★ FIX: NOW show appShell — all data is loaded, panels rendered, RBAC applied,
  // correct view navigated. No flash of default dashboard on refresh.
  $('appShell').style.display = 'grid';
  $('appShell').classList.remove('no-sidebar');

  // Draw charts after appShell is visible — canvas.clientWidth/clientHeight
  // returns 0 when inside display:none parent. Use requestAnimationFrame so
  // the browser has completed layout before we measure canvas dimensions.
  requestAnimationFrame(() => { drawCharts(); });

  // Dismiss the loading screen — app is fully ready
  Loader.setStage('Session established');
  Loader.dismiss();
}

function handleLogout() {
  closeUserMenu();
  showConfirm('Sign Out','Are you sure you want to end your session?',()=>{
    addAuditEntry('LOGOUT','Session',STATE.currentUser?.username||'',STATE.currentUser?.fullName+' logged out.');
    clearInterval(STATE.sessionTimerInterval);
    clearInterval(STATE.autoDeductInterval);
    stopNotificationPolling();
    stopAuditAutoRefresh();
    stopBranchAutoRefresh();
    // Save token for logout call, then clear local state immediately
    const savedToken = authToken || localStorage.getItem('atlas_token') || '';
    authToken = '';
    csrfToken = '';
    STATE.currentUser = null;
    localStorage.removeItem('atlas_token');
    try { localStorage.removeItem('atlas_branch'); localStorage.removeItem('atlas_view'); } catch(e) {}
    API_CONNECTED = true;
    // Show login screen immediately — no navigate() since currentUser is null
    $('appShell').style.display = 'none';
    $('loginScreen').style.display = 'flex';
    $('loginForm').reset();
    // Fire-and-forget logout to server (skip mutation queue — no CSRF needed for session destroy)
    try {
      fetch(API_BASE + 'auth', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: {
          'X-HTTP-Method-Override': 'DELETE',
          'Content-Type': 'application/json',
          'Authorization': 'Bearer ' + savedToken
        },
        credentials: 'include'
      }).catch(() => {});
    } catch(e) {}
    showToast('info','Session ended','You have been signed out successfully.');
  });
}

function startSessionTimer() {
  // Read session timeout from DB settings if available, otherwise use STATE default
  const dbTimeout = DB.settings?.find(x => x.key === 'security.session_timeout');
  if (dbTimeout && parseInt(dbTimeout.value) > 0) {
    STATE.sessionTimeout = parseInt(dbTimeout.value) * 60;
  }
  STATE.sessionRemaining = STATE.sessionTimeout;
  clearInterval(STATE.sessionTimerInterval);
  STATE.sessionTimerInterval = setInterval(()=>{
    STATE.sessionRemaining--;
    const m = Math.floor(STATE.sessionRemaining/60);
    const s = STATE.sessionRemaining%60;
    $('sessionTimer').textContent = `Session: ${String(m).padStart(2,'0')}:${String(s).padStart(2,'0')}`;
    if (STATE.sessionRemaining <= 300) $('sessionTimer').style.color = 'var(--danger)';
    else if (STATE.sessionRemaining <= 900) $('sessionTimer').style.color = 'var(--warn)';
    if (STATE.sessionRemaining <= 0) {
      clearInterval(STATE.sessionTimerInterval);
      showToast('warning','Session Expired','Your session has timed out. Please sign in again.');
      handleLogout();
    }
  }, 1000);
}

/* ================================================================
   ROLE-BASED ACCESS CONTROL
   ================================================================ */
const MODULE_VIEW_MAP = {
  'Dashboard': 'dashboard',
  'Customers': 'customers',
  'Accounts': 'accounts',
  'Transactions': 'transactions',
  'Loans': 'loans',
  'Approvals': 'approvals',
  'Expenses': 'expenses',
  'Operating Fund': 'operating',
  'GL Accounts': 'glaccounts',
  'Internal Audit': 'internalaudit',
  'Branch Management': 'branches',
  'Staff': 'staff',
  'Settings': 'settings',
  'Documents': 'documents',
  'Financial Reports': 'reports',
  'Profit & Loss': 'profitloss',
  'Balance Sheet': 'balancesheet',
  'Cash Flow': 'cashflow',
  'Financial Ratios': 'ratios',
  'Audit Log': 'audit'
};

/* Actions (not views) that require ADMIN role.
   These buttons are hidden from non-admin users.
   Non-admin users can still VIEW these pages if they have the module granted,
   but they cannot create/edit/delete — the render functions handle read-only mode. */
const ADMIN_ONLY_ACTIONS = new Set([
  'action-add-staff','action-add-branch','action-fund-transfer','action-journal-entry',
  'action-credit-fund','action-record-expense','action-add-finding'
]);

/**
 * Map a frontend module display name to its backend RBAC keys.
 * Used at RUNTIME for permission checks (e.g. when checking whether the
 * current user can access a specific API endpoint).
 */
function getModuleRbacKeys(displayName) {
  const map = {
    'Dashboard':          ['DASHBOARD'],
    'Customers':          ['CUSTOMERS'],
    'Accounts':           ['ACCOUNTS'],
    'Transactions':       ['TRANSACTIONS'],
    'Loans':              ['LOANS'],
    'Approvals':          ['APPROVALS'],
    'Expenses':           ['EXPENSES'],
    'Operating Fund':     ['ACCOUNTS','OPERATING'],
    'GL Accounts':        ['ACCOUNTS','GLACCOUNTS','CHART_OF_ACCOUNTS'],
    'Internal Audit':     ['AUDIT','INTERNALAUDIT'],
    'Branch Management':  ['BRANCHES'],
    'Staff':              ['STAFF'],
    'Staff Management':   ['STAFF'],
    'Settings':           ['SETTINGS'],
    'Documents':          ['DOCUMENTS'],
    'Financial Reports':  ['REPORTS'],
    'Profit & Loss':      ['PROFITLOSS'],
    'Balance Sheet':      ['REPORTS','PROFITLOSS'],
    'Cash Flow':          ['REPORTS','CASHFLOW'],
    'Financial Ratios':   ['REPORTS','RATIOS'],
    'Audit Log':          ['AUDIT'],
  };
  // ★ FIX (CUST-015): Guard against non-string displayName (e.g. number, undefined)
  return map[displayName] || [String(displayName || '').toUpperCase()];
}

/**
 * Reverse map: RBAC key (as stored in DB) → checkbox display name.
 * Used when loading staff modules from the database to correctly
 * pre-check the right checkboxes in the edit modal.
 *
 * Handles both legacy RBAC keys (e.g. 'ACCOUNTS', 'OPERATING')
 * and new display names (e.g. 'Accounts', 'Operating Fund').
 */
const RBAC_KEY_TO_CHECKBOX = {
  'DASHBOARD':'Dashboard',
  'CUSTOMERS':'Customers',
  'ACCOUNTS':'Accounts',
  'TRANSACTIONS':'Transactions',
  'LOANS':'Loans',
  'APPROVALS':'Approvals',
  'EXPENSES':'Expenses',
  'OPERATING':'Operating Fund',
  'OPERATING_FUND':'Operating Fund',
  'GLACCOUNTS':'GL Accounts',
  'GL_ACCOUNTS':'GL Accounts',
  'CHART_OF_ACCOUNTS':'GL Accounts',
  'INTERNALAUDIT':'Internal Audit',
  'INTERNAL_AUDIT':'Internal Audit',
  'BRANCHES':'Branch Management',
  'BRANCH MANAGEMENT':'Branch Management',
  'STAFF':'Staff',
  'STAFF MANAGEMENT':'Staff',
  'SETTINGS':'Settings',
  'DOCUMENTS':'Documents',
  'REPORTS':'Financial Reports',
  'FINANCIAL REPORTS':'Financial Reports',
  'PROFITLOSS':'Profit & Loss',
  'PROFIT & LOSS':'Profit & Loss',
  'BALANCESHEET':'Balance Sheet',
  'BALANCE SHEET':'Balance Sheet',
  'CASHFLOW':'Cash Flow',
  'CASH FLOW':'Cash Flow',
  'RATIOS':'Financial Ratios',
  'FINANCIAL RATIOS':'Financial Ratios',
  'AUDIT':'Audit Log',
  'AUDIT LOG':'Audit Log',
  'ALL':'All Modules',
};

/* Reverse map: RBAC key → view ID (used for module-based nav visibility) */
const RBAC_VIEW_MAP = {};
Object.entries({
  'DASHBOARD':'dashboard','CUSTOMERS':'customers','ACCOUNTS':'accounts',
  'TRANSACTIONS':'transactions','LOANS':'loans','APPROVALS':'approvals',
  'EXPENSES':'expenses','OPERATING':'operating','GLACCOUNTS':'glaccounts',
  'CHART_OF_ACCOUNTS':'glaccounts','INTERNALAUDIT':'internalaudit',
