  // Extract a clean, short title from the description for the table
  const getTitle = (desc) => {
    if (!desc) return '—';
    // Remove [AUTO-Cxx] or [AUTO-DSxx] tag
    const clean = desc.replace(/\[AUTO-[\w-]+\]\s*/, '').split('\n')[0].trim();
    return clean.length > 80 ? clean.substring(0, 77) + '...' : clean;
  };

  $('auditFindingsBody').innerHTML = items.length ? items.map(f => `<tr>
    <td class="td-mono" style="font-size:.78rem">AF-${String(f.id).padStart(3,'0')}</td>
    <td><span class="risk-indicator ${sevPills[f.severity]||'ri-low'}" style="font-size:.68rem">● ${f.severity}</span></td>
    <td style="font-size:.82rem">${f.category}</td>
    <td style="font-size:.82rem;max-width:280px;overflow:hidden;text-overflow:ellipsis;white-space:nowrap" title="${(f.description||'').replace(/"/g,'&quot;')}">${getTitle(f.description)}</td>
    <td style="font-size:.8rem;color:var(--muted)">${f.branch||'—'}</td>
    <td style="font-size:.82rem">${f.assignee === 'Internal Audit System' ? '<span style="color:var(--accent)">'+IC('bot',14)+' Auto</span>' : (f.assignee||'—')}</td>
    <td><span class="pill pill-${f.status==='OPEN'?'pending':f.status==='IN_PROGRESS'?'review':f.status==='RESOLVED'?'approved':'active'}" style="font-size:.7rem">${(f.status||'').replace('_',' ')}</span></td>
    <td style="font-size:.8rem">${f.created_date || f.timestamp ? (f.created_date || f.timestamp).slice(0,10) : '—'}</td>
    <td class="td-actions">
      <button class="btn btn-sm btn-ghost" onclick="openFindingDetail(${f.id})">View</button>
      ${f.status==='OPEN'?`<button class="btn btn-sm btn-primary" onclick="updateFindingStatus(${f.id},'IN_PROGRESS')">Start</button>`:''}
      ${f.status==='IN_PROGRESS'?`<button class="btn btn-sm btn-primary" onclick="updateFindingStatus(${f.id},'RESOLVED')">Resolve</button>`:''}
      ${f.status==='RESOLVED'?`<button class="btn btn-sm btn-primary" onclick="updateFindingStatus(${f.id},'CLOSED')">Close</button>`:''}
    </td>
  </tr>`).join('') : '<tr><td colspan="9" class="empty-state"><p>No findings match your filters</p><p style="font-size:.75rem;color:var(--muted);margin-top:4px">Try changing the filter options above, or click "Refresh Dashboard" to run a new audit scan.</p></td></tr>';
  renderPagination('auditFindingsPagination', STATE.auditPage, filtered.length, 'goAuditFindingsPage', STATE.auditPageSize);
}

function goAuditFindingsPage(p) { STATE.auditPage = p; renderAuditFindings(); }

/**
 * Open finding detail drawer — reads from STATE.auditFindings
 */
function openFindingDetail(id) {
  const f = STATE.auditFindings.find(x => x.id === id);
  if (!f) return;
  const sevColors = {CRITICAL:'var(--danger)',HIGH:'#ff6b86',MEDIUM:'var(--warn)',LOW:'var(--muted)'};
  const isAuto = f.description && f.description.includes('[AUTO-');
  const autoTag = isAuto ? '<span style="background:linear-gradient(135deg,#6366f1,#8b5cf6);color:#fff;font-size:.68rem;padding:3px 8px;border-radius:20px;font-weight:600;margin-left:8px">Auto-Generated</span>' : '';

  // Format description: convert newlines to HTML paragraphs for readability
  const descHtml = (f.description || 'No description provided.')
    .split('\n').filter(l => l.trim()).map(l => {
      // Check if this line looks like a header (starts with [AUTO- or is short and bold-like)
      const isHeader = l.startsWith('[AUTO-') || (l.length < 80 && l === l.toUpperCase() && l.trim().length > 0);
      return isHeader
        ? `<div style="font-weight:700;font-size:.9rem;color:var(--text);margin-bottom:8px">${l.replace(/\[AUTO-[\w-]+\]\s*/, '')}</div>`
        : `<p style="font-size:.84rem;color:var(--muted);line-height:1.7;margin:0 0 6px 0">${l}</p>`;
    }).join('');

  // Format recommendation: parse WHAT/WHY/HOW/WHO sections
  const recText = f.recommendation || 'No recommendation provided.';
  const recSections = recText.split(/\n(?=(?:WHAT|WHY|HOW|WHO)\s*[:：])/);
  let recHtml = '';
  if (recSections.length > 1) {
    // Structured recommendation with WHAT/WHY/HOW/WHO
    recHtml = recSections.map(section => {
      const lines = section.trim().split('\n');
      const header = lines[0].trim();
      const body = lines.slice(1).join(' ');
      const headerColor = header.startsWith('WHAT') ? 'var(--text)' : header.startsWith('WHY') ? 'var(--danger)' : header.startsWith('HOW') ? 'var(--accent)' : 'var(--primary)';
      return `<div style="margin-bottom:10px">
        <div style="font-weight:700;font-size:.8rem;color:${headerColor};margin-bottom:4px">${header}</div>
        <div style="font-size:.82rem;color:var(--muted);line-height:1.7">${body}</div>
      </div>`;
    }).join('');
  } else {
    // Unstructured recommendation — render as paragraph
    recHtml = `<p style="font-size:.84rem;color:var(--muted);line-height:1.7">${recText}</p>`;
  }

  openDrawer('Finding AF-' + String(f.id).padStart(3, '0'), `
    <div class="drawer-section"><h4>Finding Details ${autoTag}</h4>
      <div class="detail-grid">
        <div class="detail-item"><div class="di-label">Severity</div><div class="di-value" style="color:${sevColors[f.severity]||'var(--muted)'};font-weight:800;font-size:1.1rem">${f.severity}</div></div>
        <div class="detail-item"><div class="di-label">Status</div><div class="di-value"><span class="pill pill-${f.status==='OPEN'?'pending':f.status==='IN_PROGRESS'?'review':f.status==='RESOLVED'?'approved':'active'}">${(f.status||'').replace('_',' ')}</span></div></div>
        <div class="detail-item"><div class="di-label">Category</div><div class="di-value">${f.category}</div></div>
        <div class="detail-item"><div class="di-label">Branch</div><div class="di-value">${f.branch||'—'}</div></div>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="drawer-section"><h4>What Happened</h4>${descHtml}</div>
    <div class="drawer-section"><h4>How to Fix It</h4>${recHtml}</div>
    <div class="drawer-section"><h4>Audit Trail</h4>
      <div class="detail-row"><span class="label">Assigned To</span><span class="value">${f.assignee==='Internal Audit System'?'<span style="color:var(--accent)">Internal Audit System (Auto)</span>':(f.assignee||'—')}</span></div>
      <div class="detail-row"><span class="label">Created By</span><span class="value">${f.created_by||'—'}</span></div>
      <div class="detail-row"><span class="label">Created Date</span><span class="value">${f.created_date || (f.timestamp ? f.timestamp.slice(0,10) : '—')}</span></div>
      <div class="detail-row"><span class="label">Last Updated</span><span class="value">${f.updated_at ? f.updated_at.slice(0,19) : '—'}</span></div>
    </div>
    <div style="display:flex;gap:8px;margin-top:16px">
      ${f.status==='OPEN'?`<button class="btn btn-primary" onclick="updateFindingStatus(${f.id},'IN_PROGRESS');closeDrawer()">Start Remediation</button>`:''}
      ${f.status==='IN_PROGRESS'?`<button class="btn btn-primary" onclick="updateFindingStatus(${f.id},'RESOLVED');closeDrawer()">Mark Resolved</button>`:''}
      ${f.status==='RESOLVED'?`<button class="btn btn-primary" onclick="updateFindingStatus(${f.id},'CLOSED');closeDrawer()">Close Finding</button>`:''}
    </div>`);
}

/**
 * Update finding status — calls PUT /api/audit/findings/:id on backend
 */
async function updateFindingStatus(id, status) {
  const res = await API.put('audit/findings/' + id, {status});
  if (res) {
    showToast('success', 'Finding Updated', `Finding #${id} status changed to ${status.replace('_',' ')}.`);
    // Update local cache
    const f = STATE.auditFindings.find(x => x.id === id);
    if (f) f.status = status;
    renderInternalAudit();
  }
}

/**
 * Add finding handler — calls POST /api/audit/findings on backend
 */
async function handleAddFinding(e) {
  e.preventDefault();
  const payload = {
    severity: $('afSeverity').value,
    category: $('afCategory').value,
    branch: $('afBranch').value,
    description: $('afDescription').value.trim(),
    recommendation: $('afRecommendation').value.trim(),
    assignee: $('afAssignee').value.trim()
  };
  const res = await API.post('audit/findings', payload);
  if (res) {
    closeModal('addFindingModal');
    $('addFindingForm').reset();
    showToast('success', 'Finding Added', `Audit finding has been recorded.`);
    // Reload findings from database
    const findRes = await API.get('audit/findings', {pageSize: 500});
    if (findRes?.data) STATE.auditFindings = findRes.data;
    renderInternalAudit();
  }
}

/**
 * ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 * FULLY AUTOMATED AUDIT FINDINGS ENGINE
 * ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 * This engine continuously monitors the 20 compliance checks and
 * automatically creates detailed findings for every issue detected.
 * It also auto-closes findings when issues are resolved.
 *
 * Dedup: Uses [AUTO-Cxx] tags in descriptions to match existing findings.
 * Runs on: every loadAuditDashboard() call and every 15s auto-refresh poll.
 * ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 */

// Category mapping — maps each compliance check ID to a findings category
const AUTO_FINDING_CATEGORY_MAP = {
  'C01': 'Reconciliation',
  'C02': 'Compliance',
  'C03': 'Transaction Monitoring',
  'C04': 'Transaction Monitoring',
  'C05': 'Transaction Monitoring',
  'C06': 'Compliance',
  'C07': 'Risk Management',
  'C08': 'Loan Portfolio',
  'C09': 'Transaction Monitoring',
  'C10': 'Expense Management',
  'C11': 'Reconciliation',
  'C12': 'Access Control',
  'C13': 'Compliance',
  'C14': 'Risk Management',
  'C15': 'Compliance',
  'C16': 'Compliance',
  'C17': 'Risk Management',
  'C18': 'Loan Portfolio',
  'C19': 'Risk Management',
  'C20': 'Expense Management'
};

// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
// ENTERPRISE-GRADE RECOMMENDATION TEMPLATES
// Each recommendation is written in plain, clear language that any staff
// member — from tellers to executives — can understand and act on.
// Structure: WHAT to do → WHY it matters → HOW to do it → WHO should handle it
// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
const AUTO_FINDING_RECOMMENDATIONS = {
  'C01': `WHAT: The total debits and credits in your General Ledger do not match. This means your books are out of balance.

WHY IT MATTERS: A trial balance that does not balance indicates errors in how transactions were recorded. This could hide financial losses, misstated positions, or compliance violations.

HOW TO FIX: (1) Run a GL report and compare total debits against total credits. (2) Review all journal entries posted since the last time the books were balanced. (3) Look for entries with only one side (debit without credit or vice versa). (4) Correct any errors by posting reversing or adjusting entries. (5) Re-verify the trial balance after each correction.

WHO: Head of Accounts / Chief Accountant`,
  'C02': `WHAT: Some journal entries in the General Ledger have no debit or credit amount recorded. Every GL entry must have at least one debit and one credit (double-entry bookkeeping).

WHY IT MATTERS: Entries without amounts break the integrity of your financial records. They can cause the trial balance to be incorrect and make it impossible to produce accurate financial statements.

HOW TO FIX: (1) Identify all entries with missing debit or credit values from the GL Integrity table. (2) Determine what transaction each entry belongs to. (3) Post the missing debit or credit amount. (4) If the entry is a duplicate or error, delete it with proper authorization. (5) Check the transaction posting system to prevent this from happening again.

WHO: Head of Accounts / IT Systems Administrator`,
  'C03': `WHAT: One or more customer accounts have a negative balance. In normal operations, customer accounts should not go below zero unless the account has an overdraft facility.

WHY IT MATTERS: Negative balances can indicate unauthorized overdrafts, processing errors, or fraud. They also mean the bank is effectively extending credit without proper approval, documentation, or interest charges.

HOW TO FIX: (1) Identify each account with a negative balance from the check details. (2) Determine the cause — was it a processing error, an unauthorized withdrawal, or a missing deposit? (3) If it is an error, post a correcting entry to restore the balance. (4) If it is unauthorized, freeze the account and escalate to the fraud/compliance team. (5) Implement a real-time check to block debits that would push an account below zero.

WHO: Branch Operations Manager / Compliance Officer`,
  'C04': `WHAT: Some transactions in the system reference account numbers that do not exist in your accounts database. These are called "orphaned references."

WHY IT MATTERS: Transactions linked to non-existent accounts mean money may have moved to or from accounts that cannot be tracked. This creates audit trail gaps and could indicate data corruption, deleted accounts with active transactions, or fraud.

HOW TO FIX: (1) Export the list of orphaned transactions from the check details. (2) For each transaction, determine if the account number was mistyped, if the account was closed/deleted, or if the transaction is fraudulent. (3) Re-link transactions to the correct accounts where possible. (4) Reverse any transactions that cannot be resolved. (5) Add a validation rule so future transactions can only reference active accounts.

WHO: IT Systems Administrator / Head of Operations`,
  'C05': `WHAT: Two or more transactions share the same reference number. Every transaction reference must be unique to maintain a clear audit trail.

WHY IT MATTERS: Duplicate references make it impossible to trace individual transactions during an audit or customer dispute. They can also hide cases where the same transaction was recorded twice, leading to incorrect balances.

HOW TO FIX: (1) Review each group of transactions with the same reference. (2) Determine if they are actual duplicates (same amount, same accounts, same date) or legitimate reuse of a reference number. (3) For actual duplicates, reverse the extra entry. (4) For reused references, assign new unique references. (5) Configure the system to auto-generate unique references and block manual entry of existing ones.

WHO: Head of Operations / IT Systems Administrator`,
  'C06': `WHAT: Some customers have incomplete Know Your Customer (KYC) records. KYC is a regulatory requirement that helps verify customer identity and prevent money laundering.

WHY IT MATTERS: Incomplete KYC is a regulatory violation that can result in fines, sanctions, or license suspension. It also increases the risk of fraud and money laundering.

HOW TO FIX: (1) Generate a list of all customers with incomplete KYC from the Customer Management module. (2) Contact each customer to request missing documents (ID card, proof of address, etc.). (3) Set a deadline — typically 30 days — for customers to provide the required documents. (4) If the deadline passes without compliance, restrict the account (limit transactions or freeze it). (5) Update the account opening process to enforce mandatory KYC fields before activation.

WHO: Compliance Officer / Branch Operations Manager`,
  'C07': `WHAT: Some accounts have been marked as DORMANT (no activity for a long time) or FROZEN (blocked for a specific reason). These accounts require periodic review.

WHY IT MATTERS: Dormant accounts that are not properly managed can be exploited for money laundering. Frozen accounts must have valid reasons and proper documentation to satisfy regulators.

HOW TO FIX: (1) Review each dormant account — contact the customer and ask if they still want the account. (2) If the customer cannot be reached after multiple attempts, follow the bank's dormancy policy to formally declare the account dormant and transfer unclaimed funds to the central bank per regulation. (3) For frozen accounts, verify that the freeze reason is documented and still valid. (4) If a freeze is no longer justified, unfreeze the account. (5) Schedule a quarterly review of all dormant and frozen accounts.

WHO: Compliance Officer / Branch Operations Manager`,
  'C08': `WHAT: One or more loans are delinquent — the borrower has missed scheduled repayment deadlines. Delinquent loans represent a direct financial risk to the bank.

WHY IT MATTERS: Delinquent loans reduce the bank's income, increase provisioning costs, and can lead to write-offs if not managed. High delinquency rates also signal weakness in credit underwriting.

HOW TO FIX: (1) Review each delinquent loan — check the borrower's payment history, collateral, and communication records. (2) Contact each borrower to understand why payments are late and negotiate a repayment plan. (3) For high-value delinquencies, escalate to the collections team or legal department. (4) Assess collateral coverage — if insufficient, increase provisions. (5) Review the loan origination process to identify underwriting weaknesses that allowed these delinquencies.

WHO: Credit Risk Manager / Head of Lending`,
  'C09': `WHAT: Transactions exceeding 5,000,000 FCFA were detected. Large transactions require enhanced monitoring under Anti-Money Laundering (AML) regulations.

WHY IT MATTERS: Large transactions can be indicative of money laundering, terrorist financing, or other illicit activity. Regulations require banks to monitor, document, and report such transactions.

HOW TO FIX: (1) Review each large transaction — verify it has proper authorization, supporting documentation (invoices, contracts), and a clear business purpose. (2) Cross-reference the parties involved against sanctions lists and PEP (Politically Exposed Person) databases.   'C10': `WHAT: Some expense claims have been submitted but not yet approved. Pending expenses delay financial reporting and may indicate bottlenecks in the approval process.

WHY IT MATTERS: Timely expense processing ensures accurate financial statements and prevents cash flow disruptions. Delays can also frustrate employees and vendors awaiting reimbursement.

HOW TO FIX: (1) Review the list of pending expenses from the Expense Management module. (2) Approve or reject each expense within the bank's standard processing window (e.g., 5 business days). (3) If approvals are consistently delayed, identify the bottleneck — is it a missing approver, unclear authority levels, or lack of documentation? (4) Set up automated reminders for expenses pending beyond the SLA. (5) Ensure every submitted expense has receipts and a business justification attached.

WHO: Finance Manager / Approving Authority`,
  'C11': `WHAT: The Operating Fund balance does not match the balance shown in GL account 1400 (Cash and Banks). These two figures should always be identical.

WHY IT MATTERS: A discrepancy between the operating fund and its GL account means your cash position is misstated. This can lead to incorrect liquidity management, failed payments, or undetected losses.

HOW TO FIX: (1) Compare the operating fund transaction history against GL entries for account 1400. (2) Identify any transaction recorded in one system but not the other — common causes include manual GL adjustments not reflected in the operating fund, or fund transfers posted outside the normal workflow. (3) Post correcting entries to bring both sides into agreement. (4) Ensure all future fund movements are posted simultaneously to both systems. (5) Run this reconciliation daily as part of the end-of-day process.

WHO: Head of Accounts / Treasury Manager`,
  'C12': `WHAT: Some staff members are marked as INACTIVE but may still have system access. Former or inactive employees should not be able to log in or perform transactions.

  'C15': 'This check confirms the branch is operational and being audited. No action required unless specific branch issues have been identified in other compliance checks above.',

  'C17': `WHAT: The Loan-to-Deposit Ratio (LDR) measures how much of the bank's customer deposits have been lent out. A healthy range is typically 30% to 90%.

WHY IT MATTERS: If the LDR is above 90%, the bank may not have enough liquidity to meet withdrawal demands. If below 30%, the bank is not using its deposit base efficiently to generate interest income.

  'C18': `WHAT: Accrued interest represents interest earned on loans but not yet received from borrowers. This is an asset on the bank's balance sheet.

  'C20': `WHAT: Some expense claims were rejected during the approval process. Rejected expenses may indicate process issues, policy violations, or training needs.

(4) Provide targeted training to staff with frequent rejections. (5) Track rejection rates monthly to measure improvement.

WHO: Finance Manager / HR Training Coordinator`
};

/**
 * Main automated findings generator.
 * Examines all 20 compliance checks, creates new findings for issues,
 * and auto-closes findings when issues are resolved.
 *
 * @param {Array} checks - Array of compliance check objects from computeBranchAuditData or server dashboard
 * @param {string} branchScope - Branch name or 'All Branches'
 */
async function autoGenerateFindings(checks, branchScope) {
  if (!checks || !checks.length) return;

  // ★ FIX IAUD-003: Prevent overlapping auto-gen runs (race condition guard)
  // The 15-second auto-refresh fires autoGenerateFindings() as fire-and-forget.
  // If a previous run hasn't completed, skip this cycle to prevent duplicate
  // findings from being created by concurrent runs.
  if (STATE._auditGenRunning) {
    console.log('[AutoAudit] Skipping — previous run still in progress');
    return;
  }
  STATE._auditGenRunning = true;

  const existingFindings = STATE.auditFindings || [];
  const autoTag = (checkId) => `[AUTO-${checkId}]`;

  // ★ FIX IAUD-004: Deduplicate auto-findings by (check ID + branch)
  // Previously only indexed the FIRST non-CLOSED finding per check ID. If multiple
  // OPEN findings existed for the same check + branch (from race conditions or
  // cross-mode runs), only the first was indexed — the rest were invisible to the
  // dedup, causing the engine to sometimes skip detection of existing findings.
  // Now we index ALL non-CLOSED findings and also auto-close duplicates.
  const existingByCheckId = {};
  const duplicateFindings = []; // Track duplicates for auto-cleanup
  existingFindings.forEach(f => {
    if (f.description && f.status !== 'CLOSED' && f.status !== 'DELETED') {
      const match = f.description.match(/\[AUTO-(C\d+)\]/);
      if (match) {
        // ★ FIX IAUD-004: Use composite key (checkId + branch) for dedup
        // Previously used checkId only, which meant a finding for KUMBA could
        // prevent creating one for "All Branches" (or vice versa). Now each
        // (check, branch) combination is tracked independently.
        const key = match[1] + '|' + (f.branch || '');
        const findingBranch = f.branch || '';
        const shouldIndex = branchScope === 'All Branches'
          ? true  // Global mode: index all findings (all branches + "All Branches")
          : (findingBranch === branchScope); // Branch mode: only this branch

        if (shouldIndex) {
          if (!existingByCheckId[key]) {
            existingByCheckId[key] = f;
          } else {
            // ★ FIX IAUD-004: Track duplicate findings for same (check, branch)
            // These accumulate from race conditions or cross-mode engine runs.
            // We auto-close them in the cleanup phase below.
            duplicateFindings.push(f);
          }
        }
        // Also maintain the old key (checkId only) for backward compatibility
        // with the existing code that looks up by check.id (not composite key)
        if (!existingByCheckId[match[1]]) {
          existingByCheckId[match[1]] = f;
        }
      }
    }
  });

  // Exclude C19 (Open Critical/High Findings) from auto-generation.
  // C19 is a meta-check that counts existing open findings — generating a finding
  // about having open findings creates a confusing circular reference that adds
  // no value. C19 remains visible in the compliance checks panel for awareness.
  const checksToProcess = checks.filter(c => (c.status === 'FAIL' || c.status === 'WARNING') && c.id !== 'C19');
  const passingChecks = checks.filter(c => c.status === 'PASS');

  let newFindingsCreated = 0;
  let findingsAutoClosed = 0;

  // ── PHASE 1: Create findings for failing/warning checks (skip if already exists) ──
  for (const check of checksToProcess) {
    const existing = existingByCheckId[check.id];
    if (existing) {
      // Finding already exists — skip. It was created correctly with proper description,
      // severity, category, and recommendation at creation time. The backend PUT endpoint
      // only accepts status changes, so we don't try to rewrite other fields.
      // If severity changed, update severity via local cache only (next POST will carry it).
      const newSeverity = check.severity === 'CRITICAL' ? 'CRITICAL' : (check.severity === 'HIGH' ? 'HIGH' : (check.status === 'FAIL' ? 'HIGH' : 'MEDIUM'));
      const f = STATE.auditFindings.find(x => x.id === existing.id);
      if (f && f.severity !== newSeverity) f.severity = newSeverity; // local update only
      continue;
    }

    // No existing finding — create one
    const severity = check.severity === 'CRITICAL' ? 'CRITICAL' : (check.severity === 'HIGH' ? 'HIGH' : (check.status === 'FAIL' ? 'HIGH' : 'MEDIUM'));
    const payload = {
      severity: severity,
      category: AUTO_FINDING_CATEGORY_MAP[check.id] || 'Compliance',
      branch: branchScope,
      description: buildAutoFindingDescription(check),
      recommendation: AUTO_FINDING_RECOMMENDATIONS[check.id] || 'Investigate and remediate this finding.',
      assignee: 'Internal Audit System'
    };

    const res = await API.post('audit/findings', payload);
    if (res?.data) {
      // Add to local cache
      STATE.auditFindings.push(res.data);
      newFindingsCreated++;
      console.log(`[AutoAudit] Created finding for ${check.id} (${check.name}): ${check.status}`);
    }
  }

  // ── PHASE 2: Auto-close findings ONLY for checks that PASS in the SAME scope ──
  // IMPORTANT: Only close findings that belong to the current branch scope.
  // In global mode, don't close branch-specific findings — those belong to individual branches.
  // In branch mode, only close findings for THIS branch.
  for (const check of passingChecks) {
    const existing = existingByCheckId[check.id];
    if (existing && existing.status !== 'CLOSED' && existing.status !== 'RESOLVED') {
      // Don't auto-close if this finding was created in a different scope
      // (e.g., a global finding shouldn't be closed because one branch passes)
      const findingBranch = existing.branch || '';
      if (branchScope === 'All Branches') {
        // Global mode: only close findings tagged as 'All Branches'
        if (findingBranch !== 'All Branches' && findingBranch !== '') continue;
      }
      // Don't auto-close findings that were just created (within the last 60 seconds)
      const createdTime = existing.created_at || existing.timestamp || '';
      if (createdTime) {
        const age = (Date.now() - new Date(createdTime).getTime()) / 1000;
        if (age < 60) continue; // Skip — too new, give it time to settle
      }
      try {
        const closeRes = await API.put('audit/findings/' + existing.id, { status: 'CLOSED' });
        if (closeRes) {
          const f = STATE.auditFindings.find(x => x.id === existing.id);
          if (f) f.status = 'CLOSED';
          findingsAutoClosed++;
          console.log(`[AutoAudit] Auto-closed finding for ${check.id} (${check.name}) — issue resolved`);
        }
      } catch(e) { /* silent — auto-close is non-critical */ }
    }
  }

  // ── PHASE 2b: ★ FIX IAUD-004 — Auto-close duplicate findings ──
  // Duplicate findings accumulate when the auto-engine runs from different branch
  // scopes or during race conditions. Each (check, branch) should have at most ONE
  // active finding. Duplicates inflate KPI card counts and mislead users.
  for (const dup of duplicateFindings) {
    if (dup.status === 'CLOSED' || dup.status === 'RESOLVED' || dup.status === 'DELETED') continue;
    // Don't close duplicates that are very new (within 60 seconds)
    const createdTime = dup.created_at || dup.timestamp || '';
    if (createdTime) {
      const age = (Date.now() - new Date(createdTime).getTime()) / 1000;
      if (age < 60) continue;
    }
    try {
      const closeRes = await API.put('audit/findings/' + dup.id, { status: 'CLOSED' });
      if (closeRes) {
        const f = STATE.auditFindings.find(x => x.id === dup.id);
        if (f) f.status = 'CLOSED';
        findingsAutoClosed++;
        console.log(`[AutoAudit] Auto-closed duplicate finding AF-${String(dup.id).padStart(3,'0')} for ${dup.branch || '?'}`);
      }
    } catch(e) { /* silent — duplicate cleanup is non-critical */ }
  }

  // ── PHASE 3: Run deep-scan analysis for hidden issues beyond the 20 checks ──
  const deepFindings = runDeepAuditScan(branchScope);
  for (const df of deepFindings) {
    // Check if this deep finding already exists
    // ★ FIX IAUD-006: Also exclude DELETED findings (consistent with dedup phase)
    const exists = existingFindings.some(f =>
      f.description && f.description.includes(df.tag) && f.branch === branchScope && f.status !== 'CLOSED' && f.status !== 'DELETED'
    );
    if (!exists) {
      const res = await API.post('audit/findings', df.payload);
      if (res?.data) {
        STATE.auditFindings.push(res.data);
        newFindingsCreated++;
        console.log(`[AutoAudit] Deep scan created finding: ${df.tag}`);
      }
    }
  }

  // Log summary and ALWAYS ensure the register reflects current state
  if (newFindingsCreated > 0 || findingsAutoClosed > 0) {
    console.log(`[AutoAudit] Run complete for ${branchScope}: ${newFindingsCreated} new, ${findingsAutoClosed} auto-closed`);
    // ★ Re-fetch findings from server to get the definitive state ★
    // This ensures the register always matches the database (avoids stale local cache)
    try {
      const freshRes = await API.get('audit/findings', {pageSize: 500});
      if (freshRes?.data) STATE.auditFindings = freshRes.data;
      else if (Array.isArray(freshRes)) STATE.auditFindings = freshRes;
    } catch(e) { /* silent */ }
  }
  // ★ FIX: ALWAYS re-render the findings register after every auto-engine run ★
  // Even if nothing was created/closed, the register might be stale from a previous race condition.
  // This is a cheap operation and ensures the UI always matches the data.
  console.log(`[AutoAudit] Engine finished for ${branchScope}. Re-rendering register with ${STATE.auditFindings?.length || 0} findings.`);
  renderAuditFindings();
  // ★ FIX IAUD-002: Update KPI cards after auto-gen creates/closes findings ★
  // Previously, only the register was re-rendered. The KPI cards (Critical Findings,
  // Open Items, Risk Level) remained stale, showing pre-auto-gen counts. This was the
  // root cause of "All Branches shows 14 critical findings, register shows 1" — the
  // 13 others were auto-closed but KPI cards weren't refreshed.
  updateAuditKpiCards();
  // ★ FIX IAUD-003: Release auto-gen lock so next cycle can run ★
  STATE._auditGenRunning = false;
}

/**
 * Build an enterprise-grade, clear, and actionable finding description.
 * Written in plain language so any staff member can understand the issue.
 */
function buildAutoFindingDescription(check) {
  const checkDetails = check.details || 'No details available.';
  return `[AUTO-${check.id}] ${check.name}\n\n${checkDetails}`;
}

/**
 * Deep audit scan — goes beyond the 20 standard checks to find hidden issues.
 * Scans all branch data for anomalies that the standard checks may not catch.
 *
 * @param {string} branchScope - Branch name or 'All Branches'
 * @returns {Array} - Array of { tag, payload } objects for new findings
 */
function runDeepAuditScan(branchScope) {
  const findings = [];

  // Determine data scope
  const isGlobal = (branchScope === 'All Branches');
  const bLoans = isGlobal ? (DB.loans || []) : filterByUserBranch(DB.loans);
  const bAccounts = isGlobal ? (DB.accounts || []) : filterByUserBranch(DB.accounts);
  const bExpenses = isGlobal ? (DB.expenses || []) : filterByUserBranch(DB.expenses);
  const bTransactions = isGlobal ? (DB.transactions || []) : filterByUserBranch(DB.transactions);
  const bGlEntries = isGlobal ? (DB.generalLedger || []) : filterByUserBranch(DB.generalLedger);
  const bCustomers = isGlobal ? (DB.customers || []) : filterByUserBranch(DB.customers);

  // ── DS-01: Accounts with zero balance but active status (potential shell accounts) ──
  const shellAccounts = bAccounts.filter(a => a.status === 'ACTIVE' && a.ledgerBalance === 0);
  if (shellAccounts.length > 3) {
    const acctList = shellAccounts.slice(0, 10).map(a => a.accountNumber).join(', ');
    findings.push({
      tag: '[AUTO-DS01]',
      payload: {
        severity: 'MEDIUM',
        category: 'Risk Management',
        branch: branchScope,
        description: `[AUTO-DS01] Active Accounts with Zero Balance\n\n${shellAccounts.length} customer accounts are currently marked as ACTIVE but have a ledger balance of zero. While some of these may be newly opened accounts that have not yet received funds, a large number of zero-balance active accounts can indicate dormant accounts that were never properly reclassified, or accounts created for purposes other than legitimate banking.\n\nAffected accounts (first 10): ${acctList}${shellAccounts.length > 10 ? '... and ' + (shellAccounts.length - 10) + ' more' : ''}`,
        recommendation: `WHAT: A significant number of active accounts have zero balance. These accounts may be dormant, unused, or potentially created for illegitimate purposes.
\nWHY IT MATTERS: Zero-balance active accounts that are not properly classified can be exploited for money laundering or used to circumvent transaction monitoring. They also inflate active account counts, giving a false picture of the bank's customer base.
\nHOW TO FIX: (1) Review each zero-balance account to determine when it was opened and whether any transaction has ever occurred. (2) Contact account holders to confirm their intention to use the account. (3) Reclassify accounts with no activity for 90+ days as DORMANT per bank policy. (4) Close accounts that customers confirm are no longer needed. (5) Implement automated dormancy detection that flags accounts with zero balance and no transactions for 60+ days.
\nWHO: Branch Operations Manager / Compliance Officer`,
        assignee: 'Internal Audit System'
      }
    });
  }

  // ── DS-02: Loans with identical disbursement amounts (potential cloning) ──
  const amountCounts = {};
  bLoans.forEach(l => {
    if (l.status === 'ACTIVE' || l.status === 'DISBURSED') {
      // ★ FIX IA-007: Use l.principal (not l.amount) — loan data model uses 'principal'
      amountCounts[l.principal] = (amountCounts[l.principal] || 0) + 1;
    }
  });
  const clonedAmounts = Object.entries(amountCounts).filter(([, c]) => c >= 3).sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1]);
  if (clonedAmounts.length > 0) {
    const details = clonedAmounts.slice(0, 5).map(([amt, cnt]) => `${number_format(Number(amt))} FCFA (${cnt} loans)`).join('; ');
    findings.push({
      tag: '[AUTO-DS02]',
      payload: {
        severity: 'HIGH',
        category: 'Loan Portfolio',
        branch: branchScope,
        description: `[AUTO-DS02] Suspicious Loan Amount Clustering\n\nMultiple active loans share identical disbursement amounts. While some overlap is normal (e.g., standardized loan products), a high concentration of identical amounts can indicate cloned applications, templated lending without proper risk assessment, or batch processing anomalies.\n\nDetected patterns (top 5): ${details}\n\nTotal pattern groups: ${clonedAmounts.length}. This pattern requires investigation to rule out fraud or policy violations.`,
        recommendation: `WHAT: Several groups of active loans were disbursed with identical amounts. This clustering pattern is unusual and may indicate cloned loan applications or templated processing.
\nWHY IT MATTERS: Loan amount clustering can be a sign of fraud — borrowers may be colluding to submit identical applications, or staff may be bypassing individual credit assessments by reusing the same loan template. This exposes the bank to concentration risk and credit losses.
\nHOW TO FIX: (1) Cross-reference the affected loans for borrower relationships — same employer, same guarantor, same address, or same phone number. (2) Verify each loan went through an independent credit assessment with proper documentation. (3) Check if the disbursement amounts correspond to standard loan products — if not, investigate further. (4) Escalate to the fraud investigation team if borrower collusion is suspected. (5) Review the loan origination process to ensure each application is assessed individually.
\nWHO: Credit Risk Manager / Fraud Investigation Team`,
        assignee: 'Internal Audit System'
      }
    });
  }

  // ── DS-03: Transactions posted outside business hours ──
  const offHoursTxns = bTransactions.filter(t => {
    if (!t.timestamp && !t.date) return false;
    const d = new Date(t.timestamp || t.date);
    const hour = d.getUTCHours();
    // 6AM-6PM UTC = approximate business hours (adjust for timezone)
    return hour < 6 || hour >= 18;
  });
  if (offHoursTxns.length > 5) {
    const recentOffHours = offHoursTxns.sort((a, b) => new Date(b.timestamp || b.date) - new Date(a.timestamp || a.date)).slice(0, 10);
    const detailLines = recentOffHours.map(t => `${t.ref || '?'} — ${number_format(Number(t.amount))} FCFA at ${t.timestamp || t.date}`).join('\n');
    findings.push({
      tag: '[AUTO-DS03]',
      payload: {
        severity: 'MEDIUM',
        category: 'Transaction Monitoring',
        branch: branchScope,
        description: `[AUTO-DS03] Off-Hours Transaction Activity\n\n${offHoursTxns.length} transactions were posted outside normal business hours (before 6AM or after 6PM UTC). While some off-hours activity is expected — such as ATM withdrawals, mobile banking transfers, and automated batch processing — a high volume of off-hours transactions warrants review to ensure all activity is legitimate and properly authorized.\n\nMost recent off-hours transactions:\n${detailLines}`,
        recommendation: `WHAT: A significant number of transactions were posted outside normal business hours.
\nWHY IT MATTERS: Off-hours transactions can be a sign of unauthorized access, after-hours manipulation of accounts, or automated processing errors. While many off-hours transactions are legitimate (ATMs, mobile banking, batch jobs), the volume detected exceeds typical patterns and should be reviewed.
\nHOW TO FIX: (1) Review each off-hours transaction to determine the channel (ATM, mobile, branch, batch). (2) For branch-initiated transactions outside business hours, verify the authorization and identify the staff member who posted them. (3) If batch processing explains the volume, document the batch schedule and verify all batch jobs are authorized. (4) Implement channel-specific monitoring so ATM and mobile transactions are separated from staff-initiated ones. (5) Set up alerts for staff-initiated transactions posted outside business hours.
\nWHO: Branch Operations Manager / IT Security Officer`,
        assignee: 'Internal Audit System'
      }
    });
  }

  // ── DS-04: GL entries without descriptions (potential data quality issue) ──
  const noDescEntries = bGlEntries.filter(e => !e.description || e.description.trim() === '');
  if (noDescEntries.length > 0) {
    findings.push({
      tag: '[AUTO-DS04]',
      payload: {
        severity: 'LOW',
        category: 'Compliance',
        branch: branchScope,
        description: `[AUTO-DS04] GL Entries Missing Descriptions\n\n${noDescEntries.length} journal entry in the General Ledger lacks a description. Every GL entry should include a descriptive narrative explaining the purpose of the transaction — this is essential for maintaining a clear audit trail and for external auditors to understand the bank\'s financial records.\n\nReferences affected: ${noDescEntries.slice(0, 10).map(e => e.reference || e.accountCode).join(', ')}${noDescEntries.length > 10 ? '... and ' + (noDescEntries.length - 10) + ' more' : ''}`,
        recommendation: `WHAT: Some journal entries were posted without a description or narrative.
\nWHY IT MATTERS: Descriptions are a mandatory part of the audit trail. Without them, external auditors cannot verify the purpose of transactions, and internal staff cannot trace the business reason for entries. This can lead to audit qualifications or regulatory findings.
\nHOW TO FIX: (1) Identify all entries without descriptions from the GL Integrity table. (2) Determine the source transaction for each entry and add a proper description. (3) If the entries were posted manually, remind staff of the documentation requirement. (4) If posted automatically, fix the automation to include descriptions. (5) Add a system validation rule that prevents posting entries without descriptions.
\nWHO: Head of Accounts / IT Systems Administrator`,
        assignee: 'Internal Audit System'
      }
    });
  }

  // ── DS-05: Expenses exceeding budget thresholds (top 3 largest) ──
  const sortedExpenses = [...bExpenses].filter(e => e.status === 'APPROVED').sort((a, b) => Number(b.amount) - Number(a.amount));
  const top3Expenses = sortedExpenses.slice(0, 3);
  if (top3Expenses.length > 0 && Number(top3Expenses[0].amount) > 1000000) {
    const expLines = top3Expenses.map(e => `${e.description || e.glCode || '?'} — ${number_format(Number(e.amount))} FCFA (${e.branch || branchScope})`).join('\n');
    findings.push({
      tag: '[AUTO-DS05]',
      payload: {
        severity: 'MEDIUM',
        category: 'Expense Management',
        branch: branchScope,
        description: `[AUTO-DS05] High-Value Expense Items Detected\n\nThe following approved expenses exceed the 1,000,000 FCFA monitoring threshold. While approved expenses are not necessarily improper, high-value items require enhanced scrutiny to ensure they were properly authorized, documented, and aligned with the bank\'s budget.\n\nTop 3 largest approved expenses:\n${expLines}\n\nTotal approved expenses: ${number_format(bExpenses.filter(e=>e.status==='APPROVED').reduce((s,e)=>s+Number(e.amount),0))} FCFA across ${bExpenses.filter(e=>e.status==='APPROVED').length} records.`,
        recommendation: `WHAT: One or more approved expenses exceed the 1,000,000 FCFA monitoring threshold.
\nWHY IT MATTERS: High-value expenses are a common area for fraud, waste, and abuse. Even when properly approved, large expenses can strain the budget and should be verified against actual business needs and supporting documentation.
\nHOW TO FIX: (1) Obtain receipts, invoices, and contracts for each high-value expense. (2) Verify the approval was made by someone with the appropriate authority level for that amount. (3) Cross-reference the expense against the budget to ensure it was allocated. (4) If any expense lacks proper documentation, escalate to management for review. (5) Consider lowering the monitoring threshold if the volume of high-value expenses is concerning.
\nWHO: Finance Manager / Internal Audit`,
        assignee: 'Internal Audit System'
      }
    });
  }

  // ── DS-06: Customers with multiple active accounts (potential circumvention) ──
  const custAccountCounts = {};
  bAccounts.filter(a => a.status === 'ACTIVE').forEach(a => {
    const key = a.customerId || a.customerName || a.customer_phone || 'unknown';
    custAccountCounts[key] = (custAccountCounts[key] || 0) + 1;
  });
  const multiAccountCustomers = Object.entries(custAccountCounts).filter(([, c]) => c >= 3);
  if (multiAccountCustomers.length > 0) {
    findings.push({
      tag: '[AUTO-DS06]',
      payload: {
        severity: 'LOW',
        category: 'Risk Management',
        branch: branchScope,
        description: `[AUTO-DS06] Customers with Multiple Active Accounts\n\n${multiAccountCustomers.length} customer(s) hold 3 or more active accounts. While having multiple accounts is common for business customers or customers with different product types (savings, current, fixed deposit), a high number of accounts per customer can be used to circumvent transaction monitoring limits, split large transfers below reporting thresholds, or obscure the true volume of activity.\n\nCustomers: ${multiAccountCustomers.slice(0, 5).map(([name, cnt]) => `${name} (${cnt} accounts)`).join(', ')}`,
        recommendation: `WHAT: Some customers hold 3 or more active accounts.
\nWHY IT MATTERS: Multiple accounts per customer can be used to structure transactions below reporting thresholds ("smurfing"), circumvent daily limits, or obscure the total relationship. This is a red flag for anti-money laundering (AML) compliance.
\nHOW TO FIX: (1) Review each multi-account customer to verify the accounts serve distinct, legitimate purposes. (2) Aggregate transaction activity across all accounts for each customer to get the true picture. (3) If structuring patterns are detected, file a Suspicious Transaction Report (STR). (4) Consider implementing a customer-level transaction monitoring view instead of account-level only. (5) Set a policy requiring additional approval for customers requesting more than 3 active accounts.
\nWHO: Compliance Officer / AML Analyst`,
        assignee: 'Internal Audit System'
      }
    });
  }

  // ── DS-07: Loans disbursement without matching GL entries (funding gap) ──
  const loanDisbRefs = new Set(bLoans.filter(l => l.status === 'DISBURSED' || l.status === 'ACTIVE').map(l => l.disbursementRef || l.reference || ''));
  const glLoanRefs = new Set(bGlEntries.filter(e => e.accountCode === '1100').map(e => e.reference || ''));
  const unfundedLoans = [...loanDisbRefs].filter(r => r && r !== '' && !glLoanRefs.has(r));
  if (unfundedLoans.length > 0) {
    findings.push({
      tag: '[AUTO-DS07]',
      payload: {
        severity: 'HIGH',
        category: 'Reconciliation',
        branch: branchScope,
        description: `[AUTO-DS07] Loan Disbursements Without Matching GL Entries\n\n${unfundedLoans.length} loan disbursement(s) have no corresponding journal entry in GL account 1100 (Loans Receivable). When a loan is disbursed, the amount should be recorded both in the loan management system AND as a debit to GL 1100. Missing GL entries mean the bank\'s financial records do not reflect these disbursements, leading to understated assets and potential reconciliation failures.\n\nReferences: ${unfundedLoans.slice(0, 10).join(', ')}`,
        recommendation: `WHAT: Loans were disbursed but the corresponding entries were not posted to GL account 1100 (Loans Receivable).
\nWHY IT MATTERS: This creates a gap between the loan management system and the General Ledger. The bank's total assets are understated, the trial balance may not reconcile, and financial statements will be inaccurate. This is a serious accounting deficiency.
\nHOW TO FIX: (1) For each unfunded loan, post a correcting journal entry: Debit GL 1100 (Loans Receivable) / Credit the appropriate funding source (e.g., GL 1400 Operating Fund). (2) Investigate why the entries were missing — was it a system bug, a skipped step in the disbursement workflow, or manual intervention? (3) Fix the disbursement process to ensure GL entries are posted automatically and simultaneously with loan disbursement. (4) Reconcile the loan portfolio total with GL 1100 after corrections. (5) Add an automated reconciliation check between the loan system and GL.
\nWHO: Head of Accounts / Loan Operations Manager`,
        assignee: 'Internal Audit System'
      }
    });
  }

  // ── DS-08: Rapid successive deposits to same account (potential layering) ──
  // ★ FIX IA-008: Use t.direction === 'credit' instead of t.type === 'CREDIT'
  // Transaction type is "DEPOSIT"/"WITHDRAWAL", direction is "credit"/"debit"
  const acctTxns = {};
  bTransactions.filter(t => (t.direction === 'credit') && Number(t.amount) > 0).forEach(t => {
    if (!acctTxns[t.account]) acctTxns[t.account] = [];
    acctTxns[t.account].push(new Date(t.timestamp || t.date || 0).getTime());
  });
  let layeringSuspects = 0;
  Object.values(acctTxns).forEach(times => {
    if (times.length < 4) return;
    times.sort((a, b) => a - b);
    // Check for 4+ deposits within 60 minutes
    for (let i = 0; i <= times.length - 4; i++) {
      if (times[i + 3] - times[i] < 3600000) { layeringSuspects++; break; }
    }
  });
  if (layeringSuspects > 0) {
    findings.push({
      tag: '[AUTO-DS08]',
      payload: {
        severity: 'HIGH',
        category: 'Transaction Monitoring',
        branch: branchScope,
        description: `[AUTO-DS08] Potential Layering Activity Detected\n\n${layeringSuspects} account(s) show patterns of 4 or more deposits within a 60-minute window. This pattern is consistent with "layering" — a stage of money laundering where illicit funds are broken into multiple smaller deposits in rapid succession to obscure the origin of the money and avoid triggering transaction reporting thresholds.\n\nThis is a high-severity finding that requires immediate review by the compliance team.`,
        recommendation: `WHAT: Multiple accounts received 4 or more deposits within 60 minutes, a pattern consistent with money laundering layering.
\nWHY IT MATTERS: Layering is the second stage of money laundering, where criminals break large sums into many small transactions to avoid detection. If this pattern is confirmed, the bank may be processing illicit funds and faces regulatory penalties, reputational damage, and potential criminal liability.
\nHOW TO FIX: (1) Immediately freeze the affected accounts pending investigation — do NOT alert the account holders. (2) Review the full transaction history of each account for the past 90 days. (3) Identify the source of funds for each deposit — are they from the same originator? (4) Cross-reference account holders against sanctions lists and PEP databases. (5) File Suspicious Transaction Reports (STR) with the relevant financial intelligence unit. (6) If confirmed, cooperate with law enforcement as required.
\nWHO: Compliance Officer / AML Analyst / MLRO (Money Laundering Reporting Officer)`,
        assignee: 'Internal Audit System'
      }
    });
  }

  return findings;
}

/**
 * Run automated audit checks — now triggers full auto-findings generation
 */
async function runAutomatedAuditChecks() {
  await loadAuditDashboard();
}

/**
 * Legacy compat wrappers
 */
function getComplianceScore() {
  return STATE.auditDashboard?.compliance_score?.score || 0;
}
function getBranchAuditScores() {
  const branches = DB.branches || [];
  return branches.filter(b => b.status === 'ACTIVE').map(b => {
    const findings = STATE.auditFindings.filter(f => f.branch === b.name || f.branch === 'All Branches');
    // ★ FIX IA-002: Count only OPEN+IN_PROGRESS+ESCALATED findings for the 'open' metric
    // (matching backend by_severity and KPI card logic)
    const open = findings.filter(f => f.status === 'OPEN' || f.status === 'IN_PROGRESS' || f.status === 'ESCALATED').length;
    const total = findings.length;
    const resolved = findings.filter(f => f.status === 'RESOLVED' || f.status === 'CLOSED').length;
    const score = total > 0 ? Math.round(((total - open) / total) * 100) : 100;
    return {name:b.name, code:b.code, score, open, total, resolved};
  });
}

/**
 * Export findings as CSV — from STATE.auditFindings (database data)
 */
function exportAuditFindingsCSV() {
  const findings = filterByUserBranch(STATE.auditFindings || []);
  const branchLabel = isAllBranchesMode() ? 'All Branches' : STATE.currentBranch;
  const meta = csvMetaHeader('Internal Audit Findings Export', [['Branch Scope', branchLabel]]);
  const h = 'Finding ID,Severity,Category,Description,Recommendation,Branch,Assignee,Status,Created At,Created By\n';
  const rows = findings.map(f => `${csv('AF-'+String(f.id).padStart(3,'0'))},${csv(f.severity)},${csv(f.category)},${csv(f.description)},${csv(f.recommendation)},${csv(f.branch)},${csv(f.assignee)},${csv(f.status)},${csv(f.created_date || f.timestamp || f.created_at)},${csv(f.created_by)}`).join('\n');
  const open = findings.filter(f=>f.status==='OPEN').length;
  const critical = findings.filter(f=>f.severity==='CRITICAL').length;
  const footer = csvFooter(findings.length, [{label:'Open Findings',value:open},{label:'Critical Findings',value:critical}]);
  downloadCSV(meta + h + rows + footer, `atlas-audit-findings-${branchLabel}.csv`);
  showToast('success', 'Export Complete', `${findings.length} audit findings exported for ${isAllBranchesMode() ? 'all branches' : STATE.currentBranch}.`);
}

/**
 * Generate audit report — uses branch-specific data when a branch is selected
 */
function generateAuditReport() {
  // Branch isolation: compute data for current branch context
  let cs, fs, fh, checks, glIntegrity, findings;
  if (isAllBranchesMode()) {
    const dash = STATE.auditDashboard || {};
    cs = dash.compliance_score || {score:0, total:0, passed:0, warnings:0, failed:0, risk_level:'LOW'};
    fs = dash.findings_summary || {total:0, open:0, in_progress:0, closed:0, by_severity:{}, by_category:[]};
    fh = dash.financial_health || {};
    checks = dash.compliance_checks || [];
    glIntegrity = dash.gl_integrity || [];
    findings = STATE.auditFindings || [];
  } else {
    const branchData = computeBranchAuditData();
    cs = branchData.compliance_score;
    fs = branchData.findings_summary;
    fh = branchData.financial_health;
    checks = branchData.compliance_checks;
    glIntegrity = branchData.gl_integrity;
    findings = filterByUserBranch(STATE.auditFindings || []);
  }
  const open = findings.filter(f => f.status === 'OPEN');
  // ★ FIX IAUD-005: Recompute risk level from local findings for report consistency
  const critOpen = findings.filter(f => f.severity === 'CRITICAL' && (f.status === 'OPEN' || f.status === 'IN_PROGRESS' || f.status === 'ESCALATED')).length;
  const riskLevel = cs.failed >= 3 || critOpen > 0 ? 'CRITICAL'
    : (cs.failed >= 1 ? 'HIGH' : (cs.warnings >= 3 ? 'MEDIUM' : 'LOW'));
  const scopeLabel = isAllBranchesMode() ? 'Company-Wide' : ('Branch: ' + STATE.currentBranch);
  const branch = isAllBranchesMode() ? 'All Branches' : STATE.currentBranch;
  const b = DB.bankBranding || {};
  const stmtRef = generateDocumentNumber('AUD');
  const stmtDate = dateFmt(new Date().toISOString());

  // Register document for audit trail
  registerDocument({
    documentNumber: stmtRef, type: 'REPORT', subtype: 'INTERNAL_AUDIT_REPORT',
    accountNumber: '', customerId: 0, customerName: '',
    branch: branch, periodFrom: null, periodTo: null,
    operator: STATE.currentUser?.fullName || 'System',
    generatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    printCount: 1, lastPrintedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    exportCount: 0, lastExportedAt: null,
    summary: { totalTransactions: checks.length + (open.length||0), openingBalance:0, closingBalance: fh.operating_fund_balance||0, totalCredits: fh.total_customer_deposits||0, totalDebits: fh.total_approved_expenses||0, grossPay:0, netPay:0, totalDeductions:0, amount:0, transactionRef:'' },
    status: 'ACTIVE'
  });

  const html = `
    <div class="ent-doc">
      <!-- Enterprise Header -->
      <div class="ent-doc-header">
        <div class="edh-left">
          <div class="edh-logo">${b.logo ? '<img src="'+escUrl(b.logo)+'" style="width:100%;height:100%;object-fit:cover;border-radius:14px" alt="Logo">' : (b.bankName||'A').charAt(0)}</div>
          <div>
            <div class="edh-bank-name">${b.bankName||'Atlas Bank'}</div>
            <div class="edh-bank-sub">${b.headOfficeAddress||''}</div>
            <div class="edh-bank-sub">SWIFT: ${b.swiftCode||'N/A'} · BEAC Approval: ${b.cbnLicenseNumber||'N/A'}</div>
          </div>
        </div>
        <div class="edh-right">
          <div class="edh-doc-title">Internal Audit Report</div>
          <div>Report Ref: ${stmtRef}</div>
          <div>Scope: ${scopeLabel}</div>
          <div>Generated: ${stmtDate}</div>
          <div>Prepared By: ${STATE.currentUser?.fullName||'Internal Audit'}</div>
        </div>
      </div>

      <div class="ent-doc-body">
        <!-- Report Metadata -->
        <div class="ent-doc-section">
          <div class="ent-doc-section-title">Report Information</div>
          <div class="ent-doc-grid">
            <div class="ent-doc-field"><div class="edf-label">Scope</div><div class="edf-value">${scopeLabel}</div></div>
            <div class="ent-doc-field"><div class="edf-label">Classification</div><div class="edf-value" style="color:#dc2626;font-weight:800">CONFIDENTIAL</div></div>
            <div class="ent-doc-field"><div class="edf-label">Compliance Score</div><div class="edf-value" style="color:${cs.score>=80?'#16a34a':cs.score>=50?'#C9A962':'#dc2626'};font-weight:800">${cs.score}%</div></div>
            <div class="ent-doc-field"><div class="edf-label">Risk Level</div><div class="edf-value" style="color:${riskLevel==='LOW'?'#16a34a':riskLevel==='MEDIUM'?'#C9A962':'#dc2626'};font-weight:800">${riskLevel}</div></div>
            <div class="ent-doc-field"><div class="edf-label">Checks Passed</div><div class="edf-value">${cs.passed} / ${cs.total}</div></div>
            <div class="ent-doc-field"><div class="edf-label">Open Findings</div><div class="edf-value" style="color:${open.length>0?'#dc2626':'#16a34a'}">${open.length}</div></div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <!-- Executive Summary -->
        <div class="ent-doc-section">
          <div class="ent-doc-section-title">Executive Summary</div>
          <div style="font-size:.84rem;line-height:1.8;color:#333">
            This report presents the findings of the internal audit for <strong>${scopeLabel}</strong>. The overall compliance score stands at <strong>${cs.score}%</strong> (${cs.passed}/${cs.total} checks passed), indicating a <strong>${riskLevel}</strong> risk posture. ${cs.failed>0?`<strong>${cs.failed} critical check(s) failed</strong> requiring immediate attention. `:''}Total approved expenses of ${number_format(fh.total_approved_expenses||0)} FCFA against total loan portfolio of ${number_format(fh.total_loan_portfolio||0)} FCFA. Customer deposits stand at ${number_format(fh.total_customer_deposits||0)} FCFA across ${(fh.total_active_accounts||fh.total_accounts||0)} active accounts.
          </div>
        </div>

        <!-- Financial Health -->
        <div class="ent-doc-section">
          <div class="ent-doc-section-title">Financial Health</div>
          <table class="ent-doc-table">
            <thead><tr><th>Metric</th><th style="text-align:right">Value</th></tr></thead>
            <tbody>
              <tr><td>Trial Balance</td><td style="text-align:right;font-weight:700;color:${(fh.trial_balance?.is_balanced)?'#16a34a':'#dc2626'}">${(fh.trial_balance?.is_balanced)?'BALANCED':'NOT BALANCED'} (DR: ${number_format(fh.trial_balance?.total_debits||0)} / CR: ${number_format(fh.trial_balance?.total_credits||0)})</td></tr>
              <tr><td>Operating Fund</td><td class="edt-mono" style="text-align:right">${number_format(fh.operating_fund_balance||0)} FCFA</td></tr>
              <tr><td>Loan Portfolio</td><td class="edt-mono" style="text-align:right">${number_format(fh.total_loan_portfolio||0)} FCFA (${fh.total_loans||0} loans)</td></tr>
              <tr><td>Customer Deposits</td><td class="edt-mono" style="text-align:right">${number_format(fh.total_customer_deposits||0)} FCFA (${fh.total_active_accounts||fh.total_accounts||0} accounts)</td></tr>
              <tr><td>OPEX Ratio</td><td class="edt-mono" style="text-align:right">${(fh.opex_ratio||0).toFixed(1)}%</td></tr>
              <tr><td>Loan-to-Deposit Ratio</td><td class="edt-mono" style="text-align:right">${(fh.loan_to_deposit_ratio||0).toFixed(1)}%</td></tr>
            </tbody>
          </table>
        </div>

        <!-- Compliance Checks -->
        <div class="ent-doc-section">
          <div class="ent-doc-section-title">Automated Compliance Checks (${cs.passed}/${cs.total} Passed)</div>
          <table class="ent-doc-table">
            <thead><tr><th>Check</th><th>Result</th><th>Severity</th><th>Details</th></tr></thead>
            <tbody>
              ${checks.map(c=>`<tr>
                <td style="font-weight:600">${c.name}</td>
                <td style="font-weight:700;color:${c.status==='PASS'?'#16a34a':c.status==='WARNING'?'#C9A962':'#dc2626'}">${c.status}</td>
                <td>${c.severity}</td>
                <td style="font-size:.78rem;color:#555">${c.details}</td>
              </tr>`).join('')}
            </tbody>
          </table>
        </div>

        ${glIntegrity.length?`
        <!-- GL Integrity Analysis -->
        <div class="ent-doc-section">
          <div class="ent-doc-section-title">GL Integrity Analysis</div>
          <table class="ent-doc-table">
            <thead><tr><th>Code</th><th>Account Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Category</th><th style="text-align:right">Net Balance</th><th style="text-align:right">Total Debit</th><th style="text-align:right">Total Credit</th><th style="text-align:center">Entries</th></tr></thead>
            <tbody>
              ${glIntegrity.map(a=>`<tr${a.net_balance<-0.01?' style="background:#fff0f0"':''}>
                <td class="edt-mono" style="font-weight:600">${a.account_code}</td>
                <td>${a.account_name}</td>
                <td>${a.account_type}</td>
                <td style="color:#666">${a.category||'—'}</td>
                <td class="edt-mono" style="text-align:right;font-weight:700;color:${a.net_balance<-0.01?'#dc2626':'#333'}">${number_format(a.net_balance||0)}</td>
                <td class="edt-mono edt-credit" style="text-align:right">${number_format(a.total_debit||0)}</td>
                <td class="edt-mono edt-debit" style="text-align:right">${number_format(a.total_credit||0)}</td>
                <td class="edt-mono" style="text-align:center">${a.entry_count||0}</td>
              </tr>`).join('')}
            </tbody>
          </table>
        </div>` : ''}

        ${open.length ? `
        <!-- Open Findings -->
        <div class="ent-doc-section">
          <div class="ent-doc-section-title">Open Findings (${open.length})</div>
          <table class="ent-doc-table">
            <thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Severity</th><th>Category</th><th>Description</th><th>Branch</th><th>Assignee</th></tr></thead>
            <tbody>
              ${open.map(f=>`<tr>
                <td>AF-${String(f.id).padStart(3,'0')}</td>
                <td style="font-weight:700;color:${{CRITICAL:'#dc2626',HIGH:'#C9A962',MEDIUM:'#C9A962',LOW:'#718096'}[f.severity]}">${f.severity}</td>
                <td>${f.category}</td>
                <td style="font-size:.78rem">${(f.description||'').substring(0,100)}</td>
                <td>${f.branch||''}</td>
                <td>${f.assignee||''}</td>
              </tr>`).join('')}
            </tbody>
          </table>
        </div>` : ''}

        <!-- Certification -->
        <div class="ent-doc-cert">
          <strong>Certification:</strong> This internal audit report has been prepared for ${b.bankName||'Atlas Bank'} based on automated compliance checks, financial health analysis, and GL integrity verification. The findings and recommendations herein are intended for management review and corrective action. This document is classified as CONFIDENTIAL and is generated electronically. Any discrepancy should be reported to the internal audit department immediately.
        </div>

        <!-- Signatures -->
        <div class="ent-doc-signatures">
          <div class="ent-doc-sig">
            <div class="eds-line"></div>
            <div class="eds-name">${STATE.currentUser?.fullName || 'Internal Auditor'}</div>
            <div class="eds-role">Prepared By / Internal Audit</div>
            <div class="eds-role" style="margin-top:4px;font-size:.72rem">${scopeLabel}</div>
          </div>
          <div class="ent-doc-sig">
            <div class="eds-line"></div>
            <div class="eds-name">Chief Risk Officer</div>
            <div class="eds-role">Risk &amp; Compliance</div>
            <div class="eds-role" style="margin-top:4px;font-size:.72rem">Authorized Signatory</div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <!-- Footer -->
        <div class="ent-doc-footer">
          ${b.bankNameShort||'Atlas Bank'} Limited · Registered in Cameroon · ${b.registrationNumber||'N/A'} · BEAC Approval: ${b.cbnLicenseNumber||'N/A'}<br>
          Registered Office: ${b.headOfficeAddress||'N/A'}<br>
          SWIFT Code: ${b.swiftCode||'N/A'} · Tel: ${b.phone||'N/A'} · Email: ${b.email||'N/A'}<br><br>
          <strong>CONFIDENTIAL:</strong> This document contains privileged audit information intended solely for authorized personnel. Unauthorized use, disclosure, copying, or distribution is strictly prohibited.
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>`;

  printIsolated(html);
  addAuditEntry('AUDIT_REPORT_PRINTED','Report',stmtRef,`Internal audit report printed. Scope: ${scopeLabel}. Compliance: ${cs.score}%. Risk: ${riskLevel}. Checks: ${cs.passed}/${cs.total}. Open findings: ${open.length}.`);
  showToast('success', 'Audit Report Generated', `${scopeLabel} audit report has been prepared for printing.`);
}

/* ================================================================
   P&L UPDATES — v10 (Expense Integration)
   ================================================================ */
// Override getGLBalances to include expenses
const _origGetGLBalances = getGLBalances;
getGLBalances = function(filtered) {
  const entries = filtered || filterByUserBranch(DB.profitLedger);
  const balances = {};
  DB.chartOfAccounts.forEach(a=>{ balances[a.code] = 0; });
  entries.forEach(e => {
    if(balances[e.glAccount] !== undefined) {
      // GL 4500 (Savings Interest Expense) is an EXPENSE — it reduces net income.
      // Profit ledger entries for 4500 are expense outflows, so subtract them.
      if(e.glAccount === '4500') {
        balances[e.glAccount] -= e.feeAmount;
      } else {
        balances[e.glAccount] += e.feeAmount;
      }
    }
  });
  // Add expense balances from approved expenses (branch-filtered)
  filterByUserBranch(DB.expenses).filter(e=>e.status==='APPROVED').forEach(e=>{
    if(balances[e.glCode] !== undefined) balances[e.glCode] += e.amount;
  });
  // Operating fund — per-branch: global on "All Branches", branch net flow on specific branch
  const _opBalTxns = filterByUserBranch(DB.operatingAccount.transactions || []);
  const _opBalNet = _opBalTxns.reduce((s,t)=>s + (t.type==='CREDIT'?Number(t.amount||0):-Number(t.amount||0)),0);
  balances['1400'] = isAllBranchesMode() ? DB.operatingAccount.balance : _opBalNet;
  // Loan portfolio balance — branch-filtered (GL 1100)
  balances['1100'] = filterByUserBranch(DB.loans).reduce((s,l)=>s+l.outstanding,0);
  // Customer deposits — branch-filtered (GL 2000)
  balances['2000'] = filterByUserBranch(DB.accounts).filter(a=>a.status==='ACTIVE').reduce((s,a)=>s+a.ledgerBalance,0);
  // Loans & Advances Fund Pool (GL 1200) — source of truth is GL 1200 journal entries, NOT loans data.
  //   GL 1200 tracks the fund pool: DR (funding) - CR (disbursement to receivable).
  //   This is DIFFERENT from GL 1100/1201 (outstanding loan principal).
  const _gl1200entries = filterByUserBranch(DB.generalLedger || []);
  balances['1200'] = _gl1200entries.filter(e=>(e.accountCode==='1200')).reduce((s,e)=>s+Number(e.debit||0)-Number(e.credit||0),0);
  // Loans Receivable (GL 1201) — outstanding loan principal
  balances['1201'] = filterByUserBranch(DB.loans).reduce((s,l)=>s+l.outstanding,0);
  const totalIncome = DB.chartOfAccounts.filter(a=>a.type==='INCOME').reduce((s,a)=>s+(balances[a.code]||0),0);
  const totalExpense = DB.chartOfAccounts.filter(a=>a.type==='EXPENSE').reduce((s,a)=>s+(balances[a.code]||0),0);
  // ★ BUG FIX: Retained Earnings is GL 3100, NOT 3000
  balances['3100'] = totalIncome - totalExpense;
  return balances;
};

/* ================================================================
   P&L ENTERPRISE REWRITE — v10 (100% Database-Driven)
   All P&L data is fetched from the server. No client-side aggregation
   from cached data. Every metric, breakdown, comparison, and trend
   comes directly from the database via the reports API.
   ================================================================ */

/* Server-side P&L data cache — invalidated on filter change or refresh */
// STATE.plServerData is set to null whenever filters change

/* Fetch comprehensive P&L summary from server */
async function fetchPLSummary() {
  const params = {
    type: 'profit_loss_summary',
    date_from: $('plDateFrom')?.value || '',
    date_to: $('plDateTo')?.value || '',
    branch: $('plBranch')?.value || '',
    category: $('plCategory')?.value || '',
    account_type: $('plAccountType')?.value || ''
  };
  return API.get('reports', params);
}

/* Fetch P&L trend data from server */
async function fetchPLTrend() {
  const params = {
    type: 'profit_loss_trend',
    date_from: $('plDateFrom')?.value || '',
    date_to: $('plDateTo')?.value || '',
    branch: $('plBranch')?.value || '',
    category: $('plCategory')?.value || '',
    trend_mode: $('plTrendMode')?.value || 'daily'
  };
  return API.get('reports', params);
}

/* Fetch paginated P&L entries from server */
async function fetchPLEntries(page) {
  const params = {
    type: 'profit_loss_entries',
    date_from: $('plDateFrom')?.value || '',
    date_to: $('plDateTo')?.value || '',
    branch: $('plBranch')?.value || '',
    category: $('plCategory')?.value || '',
    account_type: $('plAccountType')?.value || '',
    search: $('plSearch')?.value || '',
    page: page || STATE.plPage || 1,
    page_size: STATE.plPageSize || 50,
    sort_by: 'id',
    sort_order: 'DESC'
  };
  return API.get('reports', params);
}

/* Refresh P&L from server — forces fresh fetch */
async function refreshPLFromServer() {
  STATE.plServerData = null;
  await renderProfitLoss();
  showToast('success', 'Data Refreshed', 'P&L data has been refreshed from the database.');
}

/* ★ Sync Fees — Triggers server-side backfill to ensure ALL withdrawal fees
   are recorded in profit_ledger. Then refreshes the P&L panel.
   This is the one-click fix for "fees show in transactions but not in P&L". */
async function syncFeesToProfitLedger() {
  const btn = $('plSyncBtn');
  if (btn) { btn.disabled = true; btn.innerHTML = '<span class="spinner" style="width:14px;height:14px;border-width:2px"></span> Syncing...'; }
  
  try {
    // Step 1: Run server-side backfill
    await refreshCsrfToken();
    const res = await API.post('reports', { type: 'backfill_profit_ledger' });
    
    if (res?.data?.backfilled > 0) {
      showToast('success', 'Fees Synced', res.data.backfilled + ' fee entries were missing and have been added to the P&L panel.');
    } else if (res?.data?.backfilled === 0) {
      showToast('info', 'All Synced', 'All withdrawal fees are already recorded in the P&L panel. No missing entries found.');
    } else {
      showToast('warning', 'Sync Complete', 'Backfill completed. Check the P&L panel for updated data.');
    }
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('[Sync Fees] Error:', err);
    showToast('error', 'Sync Failed', 'Could not sync fees. Error: ' + (err.message || 'Unknown error') + '. Ensure you have the REPORTS module permission.');
  } finally {
    if (btn) { btn.disabled = false; btn.innerHTML = IC('database', 15) + ' Sync Fees'; }
  }
  
  // Step 2: Refresh P&L panel with fresh data
  STATE.plServerData = null;
  await renderProfitLoss();
  updateBadges();
}

/* ★ P&L Pipeline Diagnostic — Runs the server-side diagnostic endpoint
   and displays results in a modal for enterprise debugging. */
async function runPLDiagnostics() {
  const btn = $('plDiagBtn');
  if (btn) { btn.disabled = true; btn.innerHTML = '<span class="spinner" style="width:14px;height:14px;border-width:2px"></span> Running...'; }
  
  try {
    const res = await API.get('reports', { type: 'profit_ledger_debug' });
    const d = res?.data;
    if (!d) { showToast('error', 'Diagnostic Failed', 'No response from server.'); return; }
    
    let html = '<div style="max-height:60vh;overflow-y:auto;font-size:.84rem">';
    
    // Profit Ledger Status
    const pl = d.profit_ledger || {};
    html += '<div style="margin-bottom:16px"><h4 style="color:var(--accent);margin-bottom:8px">Profit Ledger Table</h4>';
    html += '<div class="detail-row"><span class="label">Total Entries</span><span class="value" style="font-weight:700">' + (pl.total_entries ?? 'N/A') + '</span></div>';
    html += '<div class="detail-row"><span class="label">Total Fee Income</span><span class="value" style="font-weight:700;color:var(--success)">' + money(pl.total_fee_income ?? 0) + '</span></div>';
    if (pl.last_5_entries && pl.last_5_entries.length > 0) {
      html += '<div style="margin-top:8px"><strong>Last 5 entries:</strong></div>';
      pl.last_5_entries.forEach(e => {
        html += '<div style="padding:4px 8px;background:rgba(255,255,255,.03);border-radius:6px;margin-top:4px;font-size:.78rem;color:var(--muted)">';
        html += '#' + e.id + ' | ' + (e.category||'') + ' | ' + (e.source_ref||'') + ' | ' + money(e.fee_amount) + ' | ' + (e.branch||'') + '</div>';
      });
    } else if (pl.error) {
      html += '<div style="color:var(--danger)">Error: ' + pl.error + '</div>';
    } else {
      html += '<div style="color:var(--warn)">No entries found in profit_ledger table.</div>';
    }
    html += '</div>';
    
    // Missing Entries
    const missing = d.missing_profit_entries || [];
    const missingCount = d.missing_count ?? 0;
    html += '<div style="margin-bottom:16px"><h4 style="color:' + (missingCount > 0 ? 'var(--danger)' : 'var(--success)') + ';margin-bottom:8px">Missing Fee Entries: ' + missingCount + '</h4>';
    if (missing.length > 0) {
      missing.forEach(m => {
        html += '<div style="padding:6px 8px;background:var(--danger-dim);border-radius:6px;margin-top:4px;font-size:.78rem">';
        html += '<strong>' + (m.ref||'') + '</strong> | ' + (m.account||'') + ' | ' + (m.customer_name||'') + ' | Amt: ' + money(m.amount) + ' | Fee: <span style="color:var(--success);font-weight:700">' + money(m.fee) + '</span> | ' + (m.branch||'') + '</div>';
      });
      html += '<div style="margin-top:8px;color:var(--warn)">Click "Sync Fees" to backfill these missing entries into the P&L panel.</div>';
    } else if (missingCount === 0) {
      html += '<div style="color:var(--success)">All withdrawal fees with fee > 0 are recorded in profit_ledger. No missing entries.</div>';
    } else {
      html += '<div style="color:var(--muted)">Could not check for missing entries.</div>';
    }
    html += '</div>';
    
    // Fee Settings
    const feeSettings = d.fee_settings || [];
    const modeSettings = d.fee_mode_settings || [];
    html += '<div style="margin-bottom:16px"><h4 style="color:var(--primary);margin-bottom:8px">Fee Settings</h4>';
    if (Array.isArray(feeSettings) && feeSettings.length > 0) {
      feeSettings.forEach(s => {
        const val = parseFloat(s.value);
        const color = val > 0 ? 'var(--success)' : 'var(--danger)';
        html += '<div class="detail-row"><span class="label">' + (s.key||'') + '</span><span class="value" style="color:' + color + ';font-weight:700">' + val + '%</span></div>';
      });
    } else if (Array.isArray(feeSettings) && feeSettings.length === 0) {
      html += '<div style="color:var(--danger)">NO withdrawal fee settings found in database! This is why fees show 0 in P&L. Go to Settings > Withdrawal Taxes & Fees to configure.</div>';
    } else if (feeSettings.error) {
      html += '<div style="color:var(--danger)">Error: ' + feeSettings.error + '</div>';
    }
    html += '</div>';
    
    html += '</div>';
    openModal('P&L Pipeline Diagnostic', html, [], 'Close');
    
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('[PL Diagnostics] Error:', err);
    showToast('error', 'Diagnostic Failed', 'Could not run diagnostics: ' + (err.message || 'Unknown error'));
  } finally {
    if (btn) { btn.disabled = false; btn.innerHTML = IC('activity', 15) + ' Diagnose'; }
  }
}

/* Main enterprise P&L render function — 100% database-driven */
async function renderProfitLoss() {
  const indicator = $('plDataIndicator');
  if (indicator) indicator.textContent = '— Loading from database...';

  // ── Branch filter sync: auto-set plBranch dropdown to match header branch selector ──
  // When the user is viewing a specific branch (not 'All Branches'), the P&L branch filter
  // should automatically match so the server receives the correct branch parameter.
  const plBranchEl = $('plBranch');
  if (plBranchEl) {
    if (!isAllBranchesMode() && STATE.currentBranch) {
      // User is on a specific branch — set the dropdown to match
      const matchOption = [...plBranchEl.options].find(o => o.value === STATE.currentBranch);
      if (matchOption) plBranchEl.value = STATE.currentBranch;
    }
  }

  // Branch context indicator
  const bannerHtml = isAllBranchesMode() ? '' : `<div style="padding:10px 16px;background:rgba(103,232,181,.08);border:1px solid rgba(103,232,181,.2);border-radius:10px;margin-bottom:16px;display:flex;align-items:center;gap:10px;font-size:.84rem;color:var(--accent)">
    ${IC("map-pin", 18)}
    <div><strong>Branch: ${STATE.currentBranch}</strong> — Financial report shows data for the selected branch only. Switch to "All Branches" in the top menu to see company-wide figures.</div>
  </div>`;

  // Fetch from server in parallel
  const [summaryResp, trendResp, entriesResp] = await Promise.allSettled([
    fetchPLSummary(),
    fetchPLTrend(),
    fetchPLEntries()
  ]);

  const summary = summaryResp.status === 'fulfilled' && summaryResp.value?.data ? summaryResp.value.data : null;
  const trend = trendResp.status === 'fulfilled' && trendResp.value?.data ? trendResp.value.data : null;
  const entries = entriesResp.status === 'fulfilled' && entriesResp.value?.data ? entriesResp.value.data : null;

  // Cache for exports/print
  STATE.plServerData = { summary, trend, entries, fetchedAt: new Date().toISOString() };

  // If no server data, show informative message (no silent fallback to mock data)
  if (!summary) {
